title: Getting Started
description: New to Swapedly? Learn how to create your account, claim your $40 welcome gift card, and get comfortable navigating the platform.

articles.title: How to Create an Account
articles.summary: Sign up for a free Swapedly account in just a few steps and start buying and selling with Swap Bucks.
articles.content:
Welcome to Swapedly

Creating your Swapedly account is free, takes about two minutes, and immediately puts you on the path to earning and spending Swap Bucks — Swapedly's virtual currency where 1 SB = $1 USD in value. Whether you're here to clear out your closet, score a great deal, or both, this is where it all starts.

Step-by-Step: Creating Your Account

  1. Visit swapedly.com — Open your browser and navigate to swapedly.com. You can sign up from any device — desktop, tablet, or phone.
  2. Click "Sign Up" — Look for the Sign Up button in the top-right corner of the homepage.
  3. Enter your details — Provide your full name, a valid email address, and a secure password. Choose a password you don't use elsewhere for best security.
  4. Verify your email — Check your inbox for a verification email from Swapedly. Click the confirmation link inside it. If you don't see it within a couple of minutes, check your spam or junk folder.
  5. Complete your profile — After verifying, you'll be prompted to add a profile photo, write a short bio, and set your general location. A complete profile builds trust with other users right away.
  6. Claim your $40 gift card — Every new account is eligible for 40 Swap Bucks at swapedly.com/#/gift-card. This brings you most of the way to the 60 SB needed to unlock the marketplace — don't skip it.

What Happens After You Sign Up

Once your account is active, you'll be taken through a short welcome tour that introduces the key concepts of the platform: Swap Bucks, listings, escrow, and how transactions work. It only takes a couple of minutes and answers most of the questions new users have.

Before you can browse the full marketplace and make purchases, you'll need at least 60 Swap Bucks in your wallet. Your $40 welcome gift covers most of that gap. See Ways to Earn Swap Bucks to top up the remaining 20 SB quickly.

Tip: Refer friends after signing up to earn extra SB fast — each friend who signs up with your referral link adds 1 SB to your wallet. Twenty referrals and you've unlocked the marketplace without spending a cent.

Trouble Signing Up?

If the verification email doesn't arrive, double-check that the email address you entered is correct and look in your spam folder. If you're locked out or have an issue with your password, you can reset it at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password at any time. For anything else, our support team is happy to help.

Warning: Each person may only maintain one active Swapedly account. Creating duplicate accounts violates our terms and may result in all accounts being suspended.

faqs.q: Is it free to create a Swapedly account?
faqs.a: Yes, completely free. There are no sign-up fees. Purchase Credits are only needed when you're ready to buy an item and pay the transaction fee.

faqs.q: Do I need to verify my email before I can use Swapedly?
faqs.a: Yes. Email verification is required to activate your account and access all platform features, including claiming your welcome gift card.

faqs.q: Can I sign up with a social account like Google or Facebook?
faqs.a: Social sign-in options may be available depending on your platform version. Check the sign-up screen for available options.

faqs.q: What if I forget my password right after signing up?
faqs.a: Visit https://www.swapedly.com/#/forgot-password to reset it. You'll receive a password reset link by email within a few minutes.

faqs.q: How old do I need to be to create an account?
faqs.a: You must be at least 18 years old to use Swapedly.

articles.title: Claiming Your $40 Gift Card
articles.summary: New Swapedly users can claim a $40 welcome gift card — 40 Swap Bucks added directly to your wallet.
articles.content:
Your Welcome Gift: 40 Free Swap Bucks

Every new Swapedly account is eligible for a $40 welcome gift card — that's 40 Swap Bucks (SB) credited directly to your wallet the moment you claim it. Since 1 SB = $1 USD in value, this is a genuine $40 head start toward making your first purchase on the platform.

How to Claim Your Gift Card

  1. Create your account first — The gift card is for new users, so you'll need a verified Swapedly account before claiming. See How to Create an Account if you haven't done that yet.
  2. Go to the gift card page — Navigate to swapedly.com/#/gift-card. You can find this link in your welcome email, on the homepage, or during the welcome tour.
  3. Follow the on-screen steps — The page will recognize your logged-in account and walk you through claiming your 40 SB. There's nothing to purchase and no card number to enter for the welcome bonus.
  4. Check your wallet — Once claimed, the 40 SB appear in your wallet immediately. Head to Understanding Your Wallet if you're not sure where to find it.

What Can You Do with 40 SB?

The welcome gift puts you at 40 SB — just 20 short of the 60 SB minimum required to unlock the marketplace and start buying. Here's how to bridge that gap fast:

  • Refer 20 friends — Each sign-up via your referral link earns you 1 SB. See Referring Friends for Swap Bucks.
  • List and sell an item — Even one small sale can push you over the threshold.
  • Complete your profile — Swapedly may offer bonus SB for finishing your profile setup.
Tip: Claim the gift card the same day you sign up. Don't leave 40 free SB sitting on the table while you're exploring the platform.

Gift Card Eligibility and Terms

The $40 welcome gift card is available once per account, to new users only. It is non-transferable, has no cash value outside the Swapedly platform, and may have a claim window after account creation. Once the SB are in your wallet, they do not expire.

Warning: Only claim your gift card through the official page at swapedly.com/#/gift-card. Ignore any third-party sites or messages claiming to offer Swapedly gift codes — these are scams.

faqs.q: Can I claim the $40 gift card more than once?
faqs.a: No. The welcome gift card is a one-time offer per account. Each eligible account can claim it exactly once.

faqs.q: Do I need a credit card or payment info to claim the gift card?
faqs.a: No. The welcome gift card is completely free — no payment information required.

faqs.q: What if the gift card page says I'm not eligible?
faqs.a: This usually means the gift card has already been claimed on your account, or your account doesn't meet the new-user eligibility criteria. Contact support if you believe this is an error.

faqs.q: Does the gift card expire after I claim it?
faqs.a: The claim window may be time-limited after account creation, so claim it promptly. Once the SB are in your wallet, they do not expire.

faqs.q: I have 40 SB from the gift card — can I start buying now?
faqs.a: You need at least 60 SB in your wallet to unlock the marketplace. Earn 20 more through referrals, profile completion, or selling, and you'll be ready to shop.

articles.title: Setting Up Your Profile
articles.summary: A complete Swapedly profile builds trust with buyers and sellers — here's everything you should fill in and why it matters.
articles.content:
Why Your Profile Is Your First Impression

On Swapedly, your profile is the first thing buyers and sellers look at before deciding whether to transact with you. A complete, professional-looking profile signals that you're a real, trustworthy person — and that goes a long way in a community marketplace. Accounts with photos and bios consistently attract more offers and close deals faster than blank profiles.

What to Fill In

  1. Profile photo — Upload a clear, current photo of your face. It doesn't need to be professional, just recognizable. Avoid logos, avatars, or photos of your pet as your main profile image — other users want to know they're dealing with a real person.
  2. Display name — This is your publicly visible name. Use something recognizable, whether it's your first name, a nickname, or a selling handle you consistently use.
  3. Bio — Write 2–4 sentences about yourself. Mention what kinds of things you sell or buy, how quickly you respond to messages, and anything that helps people know what to expect from you. Keep it genuine and friendly.
  4. Location — Add your city or general region. This helps buyers assess whether local pickup is feasible for them, and helps you appear in local search results. Your exact address is never shown — only your general area.
  5. Notification preferences — While setting up, take a moment to configure how you want to receive alerts about offers, messages, and sales. See Managing Notifications.

How to Access Profile Settings

Log in and tap your avatar or name in the top navigation to open your profile. Look for an "Edit Profile" button to make changes. You can update your profile at any time — there's no limit on edits. See Editing Your Profile for a detailed walkthrough.

What Other Users Can See

Your profile photo, display name, bio, general location, active listings, and transaction reviews are all publicly visible to Swapedly users. Your email address, wallet balance, and payment information are always private. For full details on what's shared, see Your Privacy on Swapedly.

Tip: A specific, helpful bio stands out. Instead of "I sell stuff," try something like "Selling electronics, books, and home goods — fast shipper, always honest about condition." That kind of detail builds instant confidence.
Warning: Never include your phone number, home address, or personal payment details in your public bio. Keep that information private and communicate only through Swapedly's messaging system.

faqs.q: Is a profile photo required to use Swapedly?
faqs.a: It's not strictly required, but strongly recommended. Profiles without photos receive less engagement and may appear less trustworthy to other users.

faqs.q: Can other users see my exact location?
faqs.a: No. Only your general location (city or region) is displayed publicly. Your home address is never shared on your profile.

faqs.q: Can I change my username or display name later?
faqs.a: Display name changes are generally allowed from your profile settings. Username changes may be more restricted — contact support if you need help.

faqs.q: Does completing my profile earn me Swap Bucks?
faqs.a: Swapedly may offer bonus SB for profile completion as part of onboarding. Check your notifications or the Earn SB page for any active offers.

faqs.q: How long should my bio be?
faqs.a: Two to four sentences is ideal. Cover what you sell or buy, your general response time, and any details that help others know what to expect from transacting with you.

articles.title: Understanding the Welcome Tour
articles.summary: The welcome tour walks new Swapedly users through the platform's key features in about two minutes — here's what it covers.
articles.content:
What Is the Welcome Tour?

When you first create a Swapedly account, you'll be guided through a short interactive welcome tour. Think of it as a friendly two-minute orientation — it introduces you to how the platform works so you're not left figuring everything out on your own. The tour is especially helpful for users who are new to community marketplaces.

What the Welcome Tour Covers

  • Swap Bucks (SB) explained — You'll learn that SB is Swapedly's virtual currency, that 1 SB = $1 USD in value, and why the platform uses it instead of direct cash payments.
  • The marketplace — A quick look at how listings are organized, how to search and filter, and what you'll find once you're inside the marketplace.
  • Buying basics — A brief overview of how a purchase works from start to finish, including how Swap Bucks are held in escrow during a transaction to protect both parties.
  • Selling basics — An intro to creating a listing, setting a price in SB, and how you receive payment once a buyer confirms delivery.
  • Your wallet — A quick tour of where your SB balance lives, how to track your transaction history, and how Purchase Credits work alongside Swap Bucks.
  • Claiming your gift card — The tour will prompt you to visit swapedly.com/#/gift-card to claim your 40 SB welcome bonus if you haven't already.

Can I Skip the Tour?

Yes, the tour is optional — you can skip it and dive straight in. That said, completing it takes about two minutes and answers most of the questions new users ask support in their first week. If you're not familiar with escrow, virtual currencies, or community marketplaces, the tour is worth your time.

What If I Want to Revisit It?

If you skipped the tour or want to refresh your memory, this help center covers everything the tour does and more. A good place to start is What Are Swap Bucks? and How to Buy an Item. You can also explore the platform by navigating through the main menu — see Getting Around Swapedly for a full orientation.

Tip: Even if you've used similar marketplaces before, it's worth completing the tour. Swapedly's SB currency, 60 SB marketplace unlock, and escrow system work differently from most platforms — the tour explains all of it clearly.

faqs.q: Is the welcome tour mandatory?
faqs.a: No, it's optional. But completing it takes about two minutes and helps you avoid common early mistakes around Swap Bucks and marketplace access.

faqs.q: How long does the welcome tour take?
faqs.a: Most users complete the welcome tour in under two minutes.

faqs.q: Can I replay the welcome tour after completing it?
faqs.a: Check your account settings for a replay option. Otherwise, this help center covers all the same ground in more detail.

faqs.q: Will the tour show me how to claim my gift card?
faqs.a: Yes — the tour includes a prompt to visit swapedly.com/#/gift-card to claim your 40 SB welcome bonus.

faqs.q: What if I get interrupted during the tour?
faqs.a: You can typically restart or resume the tour from the beginning if you're interrupted. Check your notification or onboarding section in settings.

articles.title: Getting Around Swapedly
articles.summary: A quick orientation to Swapedly's main pages and menus so you can find anything you need without getting lost.
articles.content:
Your Swapedly Map

Swapedly is designed to be intuitive whether you're on a desktop browser or a mobile device, but knowing where each key section lives saves you time from day one. Here's a breakdown of everything in the main navigation and what each area is for.

Main Navigation Sections

  • Home / Feed — Your landing page after logging in. Shows featured listings, recently added items, and personalized recommendations based on your activity. A good place to browse casually and see what's new.
  • Marketplace — The full catalog of active listings from all sellers on the platform. This is where you search, filter, and browse to find items to buy. Note: you need at least 60 SB in your wallet to access this section. See Unlocking the Marketplace.
  • Sell / Create Listing — Tap the "Sell" button or the "+" icon (usually prominently placed in the nav bar) to create a new listing. This opens the listing creation form. See How to Create a Listing.
  • My Listings — A dashboard showing all your active, pending, sold, and paused listings. Use it to edit prices, manage inventory, and track your selling activity. See Managing Your Listings.
  • Wallet — Your financial hub. View your current Swap Bucks balance, escrowed SB, Purchase Credits, and full transaction history. See Understanding Your Wallet.
  • Messages — Direct messaging between you and other users. Coordinate shipping, ask questions about listings, and handle offer negotiations — all within the platform.
  • Profile — Your public-facing page showing your photo, bio, active listings, and transaction reviews.
  • Settings — Manage your account details, password, email, notification preferences, and Swapedly Plus subscription.

Mobile Navigation

On mobile, the main navigation lives in a tab bar at the bottom of your screen. You'll typically see icons for Home, Marketplace, Sell, Messages, and Profile. Tap any icon to jump to that section instantly.

Search Bar

The search bar appears at the top of the marketplace section. Use it to search by keyword, brand, product name, or category. For tips on getting the best results, see Searching and Filtering Listings.

Tip: Bookmark the help center at swapedly.com so you can get back to it quickly when you have a question. Most answers are just a search away.

faqs.q: Where do I find my Swap Bucks balance?
faqs.a: Your SB balance is displayed in the Wallet section. You can access it from the main navigation menu on any page.

faqs.q: How do I access the marketplace?
faqs.a: Tap or click "Marketplace" in the main navigation. You'll need at least 60 SB in your wallet to browse and buy listings.

faqs.q: Where do I create a new listing?
faqs.a: Tap the "Sell" button or the "+" icon in the navigation bar to open the listing creation form.

faqs.q: How do I send a message to a seller?
faqs.a: Open a listing and tap the message or contact button on the listing detail page. You can also access all your conversations from the Messages section in the main nav.

faqs.q: Where do I manage my Swapedly Plus subscription?
faqs.a: Go to Settings from your profile or main menu. You'll find subscription management options there, including upgrading or cancelling Plus.

title: Buying
description: Find items you love, understand Purchase Credits and transaction fees, make offers, and buy with confidence using Swapedly's transaction protection.

articles.title: How to Buy an Item
articles.summary: Learn how to find, purchase, and receive items on Swapedly using Swap Bucks and Purchase Credits.
articles.content:
Ready to Buy? Here's the Full Picture

Buying on Swapedly works a bit differently from traditional marketplaces. Items are priced in Swap Bucks (SB) — Swapedly's virtual currency where 1 SB = $1 USD in value — and a small transaction fee is paid separately in USD via Purchase Credits. Once you understand the two-currency system, the actual buying process is fast and straightforward.

Before Your First Purchase

You'll need two things ready before you can buy:

  • At least 60 SB in your wallet — Required to unlock marketplace access. See Unlocking the Marketplace if you're not there yet.
  • Purchase Credits — Used to pay the 10% transaction fee (capped at $20) in USD. Credits cost $0.49 each, with a minimum purchase of $5. See What Are Purchase Credits?

Step-by-Step: Making a Purchase

  1. Browse and search — Open the Marketplace and use the search bar and filters to find what you want. See Searching and Filtering Listings.
  2. Review the listing — Read the description carefully, examine all photos, check the condition rating, and note the delivery method (shipping or local pickup).
  3. Check the seller — Tap the seller's name to view their profile, ratings, and transaction history. See Viewing a Seller's Profile.
  4. Consider making an offer — If the price isn't quite right, you can make an offer below the listed amount. See Making an Offer on a Listing.
  5. Tap "Buy Now" — When you're ready, confirm the purchase. Your Swap Bucks move into escrow immediately — held securely until the transaction completes.
  6. Coordinate delivery — Arrange shipping or local pickup with the seller through Swapedly messages.
  7. Inspect and confirm — Once you receive the item, inspect it thoroughly. If everything checks out, confirm delivery in the app to release the seller's SB from escrow. See Confirming Delivery.

Transaction Fees

A 10% transaction fee, capped at $20, is charged to both buyer and seller separately in USD. You pay yours via Purchase Credits; the seller pays theirs independently. This fee funds platform operations and transaction protection.

Tip: Check the seller's recent reviews before buying, especially for higher-value items. A seller with dozens of positive reviews is a much safer bet than one with no history at all.
Warning: Never agree to complete a transaction outside of Swapedly — via cash, Venmo, or any off-platform method. Doing so voids your transaction protection, and Swapedly cannot help if something goes wrong.

faqs.q: What currency do I use to pay for items?
faqs.a: Items are priced and paid for in Swap Bucks (SB). The 10% transaction fee is paid separately in USD via Purchase Credits.

faqs.q: What is the transaction fee and who pays it?
faqs.a: The fee is 10% of the item's SB price, capped at $20. Both buyer and seller pay this fee independently in USD — it's not shared.

faqs.q: What if the item I receive doesn't match the listing description?
faqs.a: Do not confirm delivery. Open a dispute through the platform immediately. See our guide on What to Do If Something Goes Wrong.

faqs.q: Can I buy from a seller in a different city or state?
faqs.a: Yes — for listings that offer shipping, distance doesn't matter. The seller sets whether they offer shipping, local pickup, or both.

faqs.q: When are my Swap Bucks released to the seller?
faqs.a: Your SB are held in escrow and released to the seller only after you confirm delivery. You'll receive a notification when this happens.

articles.title: Searching and Filtering Listings
articles.summary: Find exactly what you're looking for in the Swapedly marketplace using search, filters, and sort options.
articles.content:
Finding What You Want

The Swapedly marketplace can have a large volume of active listings at any time, spanning everything from electronics and clothing to furniture and collectibles. The search and filter tools are your best friends for cutting through the noise and surfacing exactly what you're looking for.

Using the Search Bar

  1. Open the Marketplace from the main navigation. Remember, you'll need at least 60 SB in your wallet to access it.
  2. Type keywords into the search bar at the top of the page. The search works best with specific terms — brand names, model numbers, item types, or distinctive features. For example, "Sony WH-1000XM5" will return more relevant results than just "headphones."
  3. Hit search to see matching listings.

Using Filters to Narrow Results

Once you have search results, use the filter panel to zero in:

  • Category — Browse by item type: electronics, clothing, furniture, sports equipment, books, and more.
  • Condition — Filter by New, Like New, Good, Fair, or Poor. See the Condition Guide for what each rating means in practice.
  • Price Range — Set a minimum and maximum price in Swap Bucks to stay within your budget.
  • Location — Narrow results to your area, especially useful when you're interested in local pickup.
  • Shipping Available — Toggle this to show only listings that offer shipping if you can't do local pickup.

Sorting Your Results

Sort listings to find what you need faster:

  • Newest — See freshly posted listings first. Great for catching items before other buyers do.
  • Price: Low to High — Find the best deals within your budget.
  • Price: High to Low — Useful when shopping for higher-end items.
  • Nearest — If location is enabled, sort by distance for local pickup prioritization.
Tip: Try multiple search terms for the same item. Sellers describe things differently — "couch" and "sofa" may return different results even for the same type of item. Broad first, then filter down.

Saving a Search

If you're hunting for a specific item, look for a "Save Search" or "Watch" option on the results page. Swapedly may notify you when new listings matching your criteria are posted, so you can act fast.

faqs.q: Why am I not finding the item I'm looking for?
faqs.a: Try broader search terms, remove some filters, or check back later — new listings are added regularly. Also try alternate names or spellings for the item.

faqs.q: Can I filter by seller location for local pickup?
faqs.a: Yes. Use the location filter or the "Nearest" sort option to find items close to you that may be available for local pickup.

faqs.q: How do I search within a specific category?
faqs.a: Use the Category filter after running a search, or tap a category directly from the marketplace homepage to browse within it.

faqs.q: How do I clear all my filters at once?
faqs.a: Look for a "Clear All" or "Reset Filters" button near the top of the filter panel to start fresh.

faqs.q: Can I save a listing to revisit later?
faqs.a: Look for a heart, save, or bookmark icon on each listing. Saved items can usually be found in your profile or a dedicated "Saved" section.

articles.title: What Are Purchase Credits?
articles.summary: Purchase Credits are Swapedly's USD-based currency for paying transaction fees — separate from Swap Bucks.
articles.content:
Two Currencies, Two Purposes

Swapedly uses two distinct currencies: Swap Bucks (SB) for pricing and paying for items, and Purchase Credits for covering transaction fees in real USD. Understanding the difference — and making sure you have both on hand — is essential for smooth buying and selling.

What Are Purchase Credits, Exactly?

Purchase Credits are USD-denominated credits you load into your Swapedly account and use exclusively to pay the platform's transaction fee when completing a purchase or sale. They are not interchangeable with Swap Bucks and cannot be used to buy items directly.

  • Cost: $0.49 per credit
  • Minimum purchase: $5.00 (approximately 10 credits)
  • Use: Paying the 10% transaction fee (capped at $20 per transaction)

How the Transaction Fee Works

Every completed transaction on Swapedly incurs a 10% fee capped at a maximum of $20, paid separately by both buyer and seller in USD via Purchase Credits. Here are some examples:

  • Item priced at 20 SB → fee = 2 SB equivalent → approximately $1.98 in Purchase Credits
  • Item priced at 80 SB → fee = 8 SB equivalent → approximately $7.84 in Purchase Credits
  • Item priced at 250 SB → fee would be 25, but is capped at $20

How to Buy Purchase Credits

  1. Go to your Wallet from the main navigation.
  2. Tap "Buy Purchase Credits" or a similar option.
  3. Enter the dollar amount you want to load (minimum $5.00).
  4. Complete payment with a debit or credit card.
  5. Credits appear in your wallet immediately and are ready to use.
Tip: Load a buffer of extra credits so you're never caught short when you spot an item you want to buy right away. Running out mid-transaction is a frustrating delay.
Warning: Purchase Credits are non-refundable once purchased. They can only be used to pay Swapedly transaction fees and cannot be converted to Swap Bucks or cash.

faqs.q: Why can't I just pay the transaction fee in Swap Bucks?
faqs.a: The transaction fee is collected in real USD to cover platform operating costs. Purchase Credits are how Swapedly handles this separately from the SB economy.

faqs.q: What's the minimum amount of Purchase Credits I can buy?
faqs.a: The minimum purchase is $5.00, which gets you approximately 10 credits at $0.49 each.

faqs.q: Do both buyers and sellers pay the transaction fee?
faqs.a: Yes. Both buyer and seller each independently pay a 10% transaction fee (capped at $20) via Purchase Credits.

faqs.q: Do Purchase Credits expire?
faqs.a: Credits typically do not expire, but check the terms in your wallet section for any conditions. Unused credits carry over between transactions.

faqs.q: Can I get a refund on Purchase Credits I haven't used?
faqs.a: Purchase Credits are generally non-refundable. Contact support if you have a specific situation to discuss.

articles.title: Making an Offer on a Listing
articles.summary: Not happy with the listed price? Learn how to make an offer and negotiate with sellers on Swapedly.
articles.content:
Negotiating Is Part of the Culture

Swapedly is a community marketplace, and negotiating on price is a normal, expected part of the experience. If a listed price is higher than you'd like to pay, making an offer is a respectful way to start a conversation. Sellers who enable offers are explicitly open to hearing what you're willing to pay.

How to Make an Offer

  1. Open the listing you're interested in purchasing.
  2. Tap "Make an Offer" — This button appears on listings where the seller has enabled the offer feature. If you don't see it, that seller isn't accepting offers on that listing.
  3. Enter your offer price in Swap Bucks — Be thoughtful about the number. Offers significantly below the listed price (more than 30-40% off) are usually declined and can create an awkward dynamic.
  4. Add an optional message — A short, friendly note can make a real difference. Explaining that you're a serious buyer ready to complete quickly, or that you love the item, humanizes the offer and can tip the scales.
  5. Submit the offer — The seller receives a notification and has a set window to respond.

What Happens After You Submit

  • Accepted: The transaction proceeds at your offer price. Your SB move into escrow and the deal is on.
  • Declined: Your offer was too low or the seller prefers the listed price. You can try a higher offer or move on.
  • Counter-offer: The seller suggests a price somewhere between your offer and their list price. You can accept or decline the counter.
  • Expired: If the seller doesn't respond within the offer window, the offer expires automatically and you're free to try again.
Tip: Research comparable items in the marketplace before making an offer. If you can see that similar items are selling for 20-30% less, that gives you a solid, defensible basis for a lower offer.

Offer Etiquette

Low-ball offers can come across as disrespectful and may get you blocked by sellers. Keep offers within a reasonable range and always be polite in your messages. A friendly, fair offer is far more likely to result in a successful negotiation than an aggressive low-ball.

Warning: Once a seller accepts your offer, you're committing to complete the purchase. Repeatedly accepting offers and then backing out can negatively affect your standing on the platform.

faqs.q: Can all sellers receive offers on their listings?
faqs.a: No. Sellers choose whether to enable offers when creating a listing. If the "Make an Offer" button isn't visible, that listing doesn't accept offers.

faqs.q: How long does a seller have to respond to my offer?
faqs.a: Sellers typically have 24–48 hours to respond before the offer expires. If you don't hear back, the offer lapses and you can try again.

faqs.q: Can I make offers on multiple listings at the same time?
faqs.a: Yes. You can have pending offers on multiple listings simultaneously. Just be prepared to follow through on any that get accepted.

faqs.q: Can I withdraw an offer I've already made?
faqs.a: You may be able to cancel a pending offer before the seller responds. Check the offer status in your messages or activity feed for a cancel option.

faqs.q: Is the transaction fee calculated on my offer price or the listed price?
faqs.a: The 10% transaction fee is based on the final agreed price — your offer price if the seller accepts, or the counter-offer price if you accept a counter.

articles.title: How Transaction Protection Works
articles.summary: Every Swapedly transaction is protected by an escrow system that holds Swap Bucks securely until both parties confirm the exchange.
articles.content:
Built-In Protection for Every Transaction

Every single transaction on Swapedly — whether it's a $5 book or a $500 piece of electronics — is backed by transaction protection. The core of this system is an escrow arrangement: your Swap Bucks are held securely by Swapedly and only released to the seller after you confirm you received what you paid for. Neither party can access the funds during the exchange, which protects everyone involved.

How the Escrow Process Works

  1. You initiate a purchase — Tap "Buy Now" on a listing. Your Swap Bucks are immediately moved from your wallet into a secure escrow account held by Swapedly.
  2. The seller fulfills the order — The seller ships the item or arranges a local pickup. Your SB remain in escrow while this happens — the seller can't touch them yet.
  3. You receive the item — Whether by shipping or local pickup, the item arrives with you.
  4. You inspect and confirm — Check the item against the listing description and photos. If you're satisfied, confirm delivery in the Swapedly app.
  5. SB are released to the seller — Only after your confirmation does Swapedly release the escrowed Swap Bucks to the seller's wallet. The transaction is complete.

What Transaction Protection Covers

  • Sellers who take your SB into escrow but never ship or deliver the item
  • Items that arrive significantly different from how they were described or photographed
  • Delivery disputes where the seller claims delivery but you never received it

What Is Not Covered

Transaction protection only applies to purchases made entirely within Swapedly. If you agree to pay or receive payment through any outside channel — cash, Venmo, PayPal, or anything else — you lose all protection. Swapedly cannot investigate or recover funds for off-platform deals.

Tip: If something looks wrong with an item when it arrives, document it with photos immediately and open a dispute before you do anything else. Once you confirm delivery, the protection window closes. See What to Do If Something Goes Wrong.
Warning: Never confirm delivery before you've physically received and inspected the item. Confirming prematurely releases Swap Bucks to the seller and ends your protection coverage for that transaction.

faqs.q: When exactly are my Swap Bucks released to the seller?
faqs.a: Only after you confirm delivery in the Swapedly app. Don't confirm until you've fully inspected what you received.

faqs.q: What if I never receive my item?
faqs.a: Do not confirm delivery. Open a dispute through the platform and Swapedly will investigate. Your escrowed SB remain frozen until the case is resolved.

faqs.q: Is transaction protection free?
faqs.a: Yes. It's built into every transaction on Swapedly at no additional cost to either party.

faqs.q: Does transaction protection cover local pickup transactions?
faqs.a: Yes, as long as the transaction was initiated and the payment processed through Swapedly. Always complete the in-app confirmation at the meetup.

faqs.q: How long do I have to open a dispute if something goes wrong?
faqs.a: There is a window after the expected delivery date during which you can open a dispute. Check the transaction details for your specific deadline.

articles.title: Viewing a Seller's Profile
articles.summary: Reviewing a seller's history, ratings, and reviews before buying helps you make confident, informed purchasing decisions.
articles.content:
Know Who You're Buying From

Swapedly is a community marketplace, and while most sellers are genuine and trustworthy, it's always smart to do a quick check before completing a purchase. Swapedly makes this easy — every seller has a public profile packed with useful information to help you evaluate whether to buy.

How to View a Seller's Profile

  1. Open any listing from the marketplace or search results.
  2. Tap the seller's name or photo near the top of the listing detail page.
  3. You'll be taken to their full public profile.

What You'll Find on a Seller's Profile

  • Profile photo and bio — A quick sense of who they are and what they typically sell.
  • Member since date — How long they've been active on Swapedly. Longevity is generally a positive signal.
  • Completed transaction count — The number of successful transactions they've completed. More is better.
  • Star rating — An aggregate rating from verified buyers who've transacted with this seller.
  • Reviews — Written feedback from previous buyers. Pay close attention to what they say about item descriptions, packaging, communication, and delivery speed.
  • Active listings — All the items they're currently selling. Useful for seeing how they photograph and describe their items.
  • Response rate and time — If displayed, this shows how reliable they are at responding to messages.

Red Flags to Watch For

  • Account created very recently with no completed transactions
  • Few or no reviews, or reviews that mention discrepancies with item descriptions
  • Listing photos that look like stock images rather than photos of the actual item
  • Any suggestion — in the listing or via message — to complete payment outside of Swapedly
Tip: Focus on the written reviews, not just the star rating. A seller can have 4.5 stars overall but reviews that specifically mention "item not as described" — a detail worth catching before you buy.
Warning: If a seller asks you to pay via cash, Venmo, or any method outside Swapedly, that's a serious red flag. Report them using these steps and do not complete the transaction.

faqs.q: Can I message a seller before buying to ask questions?
faqs.a: Yes. Most listings have a message button. It's a great way to ask about condition specifics, get more photos, or confirm shipping details before committing.

faqs.q: Are reviews on Swapedly verified?
faqs.a: Yes. Reviews can only be left by users who completed a transaction with that seller, making them more reliable than anonymous reviews.

faqs.q: What if a seller has no reviews at all?
faqs.a: Everyone starts with zero reviews. Look at other signals: how long they've been a member, how detailed their listings are, how quickly they respond to messages, and the quality of their profile.

faqs.q: Can I block a seller I don't want to interact with?
faqs.a: Yes. You can block users through their profile page or via account settings. A blocked user cannot message you or see your listings.

faqs.q: Can I report a seller directly from their profile?
faqs.a: Yes. Look for a three-dot menu or "Report" button on the seller's profile page. See our full guide on Reporting a User or Listing.

title: Selling
description: Create great listings, price your items in Swap Bucks, take compelling photos, manage offers, and turn your stuff into SB.

articles.title: How to Create a Listing
articles.summary: List any item for sale on Swapedly in minutes — it's free to list, items are priced in Swap Bucks, and you're in control of the details.
articles.content:
Selling on Swapedly Is Free

There are no listing fees on Swapedly — you pay nothing until an item actually sells, at which point a 10% transaction fee (capped at $20) applies in USD. This makes Swapedly a low-risk way to turn items you no longer need into Swap Bucks you can spend on things you actually want.

Step-by-Step: Creating Your Listing

  1. Tap "Sell" or the "+" button in the main navigation to open the listing creation form.
  2. Add photos — This is the most important step. Upload clear, well-lit photos from multiple angles. You can typically add up to 8–10 images. See Taking Great Listing Photos for detailed guidance.
  3. Write a title — Make it descriptive and specific. Include the brand, model, size, color, or other key details. Good example: "Patagonia Down Sweater Jacket – Men's Medium – Navy Blue."
  4. Write a description — Include everything a buyer would want to know: dimensions, age, what's included in the sale (accessories, original packaging, etc.), any flaws, and why you're selling it. The more detail, the fewer questions you'll field later.
  5. Select a category — Choose the most accurate category so your listing appears in the right browse results.
  6. Choose a condition rating — Be honest. Use our Condition Guide to pick the right level. Accurate condition ratings reduce disputes significantly.
  7. Set your price in Swap Bucks — Since 1 SB = $1 USD in value, think of the price in dollars. See How to Price Your Item in Swap Bucks for smart pricing strategies.
  8. Choose delivery method — Decide whether you'll offer shipping, local pickup only, or both. This is entirely your call as the seller.
  9. Enable or disable offers — Choose whether buyers can submit offers below your listed price.
  10. Review and publish — Look everything over, then tap "Publish." Your listing is live in the marketplace immediately.
Tip: Listings with 5 or more high-quality photos and a detailed description get significantly more views and inquiries than minimal listings. Take the extra five minutes — it's worth it.
Warning: Do not list prohibited items such as counterfeit goods, weapons, regulated pharmaceuticals, or any items that violate Swapedly's content policies. Violations result in listing removal and may lead to account suspension.

faqs.q: Is it free to list items on Swapedly?
faqs.a: Yes. Listing is completely free. A 10% transaction fee (capped at $20) applies in USD only when an item successfully sells.

faqs.q: How many photos can I include in a listing?
faqs.a: You can typically upload up to 8–10 photos per listing. The more angles and detail shots you include, the more confident buyers will be.

faqs.q: Can I edit a listing after I've published it?
faqs.a: Yes. You can edit the title, description, photos, price, and other details from your My Listings page at any time before the item sells.

faqs.q: Can I offer both shipping and local pickup on the same listing?
faqs.a: Yes. Enabling both options gives buyers flexibility and can increase the number of interested buyers.

faqs.q: How long does a listing stay active?
faqs.a: Listings stay active until the item sells, you pause or delete it, or it expires per platform policy. Regularly check and refresh listings that aren't getting traction.

articles.title: How to Price Your Item in Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Set a competitive, fair Swap Bucks price that sells your item quickly while getting you the return you're looking for.
articles.content:
The Golden Rule of Swapedly Pricing

Pricing in Swap Bucks is intuitive once you internalize the key fact: 1 SB = $1 USD in value. So if you'd price something at $45 on another marketplace, you'd list it at 45 SB on Swapedly. The tricky part is finding the right number — high enough to make the sale worthwhile, low enough to actually attract buyers.

Researching a Fair Price

  1. Search similar listings on Swapedly — Browse the marketplace for the same or comparable items to see what other sellers are charging right now. This is your most important data point.
  2. Check what's actually sold — If Swapedly shows recently sold prices, use those. Listed prices tell you what sellers want; sold prices tell you what buyers actually pay.
  3. Compare condition fairly — A "Like New" item can reasonably be priced 20–40% above a "Good" condition equivalent. Be honest about where your item sits. See Choosing the Right Condition.
  4. Consider what's included — Original packaging, accessories, manuals, or extras add value. Highlight them in both the description and the price.
  5. Factor in the transaction fee — You'll pay a 10% fee (capped at $20) in USD from your Purchase Credits when the item sells. Make sure your net SB earnings meet your goal after this is accounted for.

Pricing Strategies That Work

  • Price to sell fast: List 5–15% below the going market rate. A faster sale means less time managing the listing and messaging interested buyers.
  • Price with wiggle room: If you expect to negotiate, list 10–20% above your minimum acceptable price so you can meet buyers in the middle and everyone feels good about the deal.
  • Bundle items: Group related items (e.g., a camera with lenses and a bag) for a higher total price than you'd get selling each piece separately.
Tip: Swapedly Plus members earn 1.5x Swap Bucks on every sale. If you're a regular seller, that boost can justify the $9.99/month cost very quickly. See What Is Swapedly Plus?

When Your Listing Isn't Moving

If a listing sits for a week or more with minimal interest, that's feedback. Consider dropping the price by 10–15%, improving your photos, or refreshing the description. Re-listing (deleting and reposting) can also give it fresh placement in the marketplace.

faqs.q: Can I price my item in dollars instead of Swap Bucks?
faqs.a: All Swapedly listings are priced in Swap Bucks. Since 1 SB = $1 USD in value, the conversion is always straightforward.

faqs.q: Can I change the price after the listing is live?
faqs.a: Yes. You can edit the price at any time from your My Listings page, as long as the item hasn't sold.

faqs.q: How does the 10% transaction fee affect my net earnings?
faqs.a: The fee is paid in USD from your Purchase Credits — it doesn't come out of your SB earnings. Your wallet receives the full SB sale price.

faqs.q: Should I price high and leave room to negotiate?
faqs.a: That's a valid strategy. Add 10–20% above your minimum to create negotiation room, and enable offers on the listing so buyers can make a move.

faqs.q: What if no one buys at my current price?
faqs.a: Lower the price, update your photos, or refresh the description. If the listing has been up a while, consider deleting and reposting to regain fresh visibility.

articles.title: Taking Great Listing Photos
articles.summary: Great photos are the single biggest factor in whether your listing sells — here's how to take them with just your phone.
articles.content:
Photos Do the Selling for You

On Swapedly, buyers can't hold your item, smell it, or try it on before purchasing. Your photos are the entire buying experience for them. Listings with clear, bright, multi-angle photos consistently sell faster, attract more offers, and command higher prices than listings with blurry or sparse images. Taking great photos is the highest-return investment of time you can make as a seller.

Essential Photo Tips

  • Use natural light — Position the item near a window during daylight hours. Natural light captures true colors and textures far better than overhead artificial lighting, which creates unflattering shadows and color casts.
  • Use a clean, neutral background — A white wall, a wooden floor, or a plain sheet provides a backdrop that keeps all attention on the item. Busy backgrounds (cluttered rooms, patterned carpets) are distracting.
  • Clean the item first — Wipe down surfaces, remove dust, lint, or pet hair, and make the item look as presentable as possible before shooting.
  • Photograph from multiple angles — Front, back, both sides, top, and bottom at minimum. For clothing, include a flat lay and a detail of the tag or label. For electronics, show ports, buttons, and the screen.
  • Include close-ups of any flaws — If there's a scratch, stain, wear mark, or dent, photograph it clearly. This is counterintuitive but critical: buyers who see flaws upfront are less likely to open disputes afterward, and it shows you're trustworthy.
  • Show scale — Include a hand, a common object, or a tape measure to help buyers understand the size of the item. Dimensions in the description help too.

What to Avoid

  • Blurry or out-of-focus shots — tap the screen on your phone to focus before shooting
  • Dark, shadowy images — move closer to a light source
  • Distracting or messy backgrounds
  • Only one or two photos for a complex item
  • Stock photos or images pulled from the internet — you must photograph the actual item you're selling
Tip: Most modern smartphones take excellent photos in good lighting. You don't need a DSLR camera — just natural light, a steady hand, and a neutral background. Portrait mode can also create a nice depth-of-field effect for smaller items.
Warning: Using stock photos or images that don't represent your actual item is a violation of Swapedly's listing policies and can result in your listing being removed.

faqs.q: How many photos should I include in a listing?
faqs.a: Aim for at least 5–6 photos. Include overall shots from multiple angles, any flaw close-ups, and scale reference. More is almost always better.

faqs.q: Can I use photos from the manufacturer's website?
faqs.a: No. You must photograph the actual item you are selling. Stock or manufacturer photos don't represent the real condition of your specific item.

faqs.q: What file format should my photos be in?
faqs.a: Standard formats like JPEG and PNG are accepted. Smartphone cameras save in JPEG by default, which is perfect.

faqs.q: Should I edit or filter my photos?
faqs.a: Light editing for brightness and contrast is fine. Avoid heavy filters or edits that alter how the item appears — that's misleading and can lead to disputes.

faqs.q: Can I add or replace photos after a listing is published?
faqs.a: Yes. You can edit photos from your My Listings page at any time before the item sells.

articles.title: Choosing the Right Condition
articles.summary: Accurately rating your item's condition is one of the most important things you can do as a seller — here's what each level means.
articles.content:
Why Condition Accuracy Matters So Much

Condition misrepresentation is the number one cause of buyer disputes on community marketplaces. When a buyer expects "Like New" and receives something that's clearly "Fair," the transaction breaks down. Choosing the correct condition rating protects you from disputes, negative reviews, and the stress of a failed transaction — and it builds the kind of reputation that leads to repeat buyers and fast sales.

Condition Levels Explained

  • New — Unused, in original sealed packaging with all original tags, inserts, or accessories. Has never been opened, worn, or used in any way. Appropriate only for genuinely brand-new items.
  • Like New — Used once or only a handful of times with no visible flaws whatsoever. May or may not have original packaging. Looks and functions exactly as new. If there is any visible imperfection at all, step down to Good.
  • Good — Moderate use with minor cosmetic wear such as small scratches, light scuffs, or slight fading. Fully functional with no missing parts. Flaws should be clearly photographed and described. This is the most commonly listed condition.
  • Fair — Heavy use with clearly visible wear — scratches, stains, dents, or significant fading. Still functional but obviously used. May be missing some accessories or non-essential parts. Price should reflect the condition honestly.
  • Poor — Significant damage, major cosmetic issues, or functional limitations. May have cracked components, heavy staining, or other substantial problems. Items listed as Poor should still have some utility or value, and the price should reflect the extent of the issues.

When You're Unsure

If your item falls between two conditions, always choose the lower one and use the description to explain the nuance. It's always better for a buyer to be pleasantly surprised than disappointed. A buyer who opens a box and finds the item is better than expected is a happy reviewer; one who finds it's worse than expected is a dispute.

Tip: Photograph flaws clearly and describe them honestly in the listing. Counterintuitively, this actually increases buyer confidence — it shows you're trustworthy and nothing is being hidden.

Condition and Pricing

Condition directly impacts what buyers will pay. A well-priced "Good" item may sell faster than an overpriced "Like New" equivalent. See How to Price Your Item in Swap Bucks for guidance on how to factor condition into your pricing.

Warning: Knowingly misrepresenting an item's condition — listing something as "Like New" when it has visible damage — can lead to disputes, forced resolution in the buyer's favor, and negative reviews that damage your seller reputation.

faqs.q: What if my item falls exactly between two condition levels?
faqs.a: Choose the lower condition and use the description field to note what's there and what isn't. Transparency always wins with buyers.

faqs.q: Can I change the condition rating after publishing a listing?
faqs.a: Yes. You can edit the condition from your My Listings page at any time before the item sells.

faqs.q: Does condition affect how my listing shows up in search?
faqs.a: Yes. Buyers frequently filter by condition, so accurate labeling ensures your listing appears when relevant shoppers search.

faqs.q: What counts as a flaw that I need to disclose?
faqs.a: Any scratch, scuff, stain, dent, crack, missing part, or functional limitation should be disclosed in the description and photographed. If you can see it, mention it.

faqs.q: I'm selling something brand new that I never used — New or Like New?
faqs.a: If it's genuinely unused and still in sealed original packaging, choose New. If the packaging has been opened or removed even though the item was never used, Like New is more accurate.

articles.title: Managing Your Listings
articles.summary: Edit, pause, relist, or remove your listings from the My Listings dashboard — your central hub for everything you're selling.
articles.content:
Your Selling Command Center

The My Listings section of your Swapedly account is where you manage everything you're selling. Whether you need to update a price, respond to buyer questions, pause a listing while you're on vacation, or pull an item that sold elsewhere, it all happens here.

Accessing My Listings

Navigate to My Listings from the main menu or your profile page. You'll see all your listings organized by status: active, pending (in a transaction), sold, paused, and expired.

What You Can Do from My Listings

  • Edit a listing — Update the title, description, photos, price, condition rating, or delivery method at any time before the item sells. Staying current with your listings keeps buyers' interest fresh.
  • Pause a listing — Temporarily hide a listing from the marketplace without deleting it. Useful when you're traveling, sick, or temporarily unable to fulfill orders. The listing remains saved and can be reactivated with one tap.
  • Mark as sold — If you sold or gave away an item outside of a Swapedly transaction, mark it sold here to remove it from the marketplace and keep your inventory accurate.
  • Delete a listing — Permanently remove a listing. Note: if there's an active offer or pending transaction on the listing, handle those first.
  • Relist an item — Quickly repost an expired or sold listing with the same details. Useful for sellers with recurring inventory or items you list multiple times.
  • Add a listing highlight — Boost your listing's visibility in search results. See Highlighting Your Listings.

Managing Offers

When a buyer submits an offer, you'll receive a notification. Review and respond from the listing page or the Messages section. See Accepting and Declining Offers for the full process.

Monitoring Performance

Many listing pages display view counts and the number of people who have saved the item. Use this data to gauge interest. Low views after several days may mean your title or photos need work. Lots of views but no offers may mean your price is too high.

Tip: Review your active listings every few days. Drop prices on slow-moving items, update photos that aren't performing, and respond quickly to any messages or offers — responsiveness is one of the top factors in successful selling.

faqs.q: Can I have an unlimited number of active listings at once?
faqs.a: Yes. There's no stated limit on how many listings you can have active simultaneously.

faqs.q: What happens if I delete a listing that has a pending offer?
faqs.a: Deleting the listing cancels any pending offers. Communicate with interested buyers before doing so to maintain a good reputation.

faqs.q: How will I know if someone is interested in my listing?
faqs.a: You'll receive notifications for new offers, messages, and saves (wishlists). Make sure your notification preferences are set up — see Managing Notifications.

faqs.q: Can I duplicate a listing to create a similar one quickly?
faqs.a: Check your listing options for a duplicate or copy feature. It can save significant time when listing multiple similar items.

faqs.q: What happens to a listing if no one buys it after a long time?
faqs.a: Listings may expire after a set period per platform policy. You'll be notified if this happens and can relist with a fresh post.

articles.title: Accepting and Declining Offers
articles.summary: Learn how to review, accept, counter, or decline buyer offers on your Swapedly listings.
articles.content:
When a Buyer Makes an Offer

If you've enabled offers on a listing, buyers can propose a price below your listed amount. You'll receive a notification when an offer comes in, and you have a set response window — typically 24–48 hours — before the offer expires. Responding promptly is important: buyers with active offers often have multiple listings in mind, and a slow response can cost you a sale.

How to Review and Respond to an Offer

  1. Open the notification — Tap the offer notification or find it in your messages or offers inbox.
  2. Review the details — See the buyer's proposed price, check their profile and transaction history, and read any note they included with the offer.
  3. Choose your response:
  • Accept — You agree to the buyer's price. Their Swap Bucks immediately move into escrow and the transaction proceeds at the offer price. This is binding — be sure before you accept.
  • Decline — You're not interested at that price. The buyer's offer is rejected, and they may choose to offer a higher amount, buy at the listed price, or move on.
  • Counter-offer — You propose a different price, typically somewhere between the buyer's offer and your listed price. The buyer can then accept or decline your counter. Counter-offers are a great tool for keeping a negotiation alive without accepting a price you're unhappy with.

Handling Multiple Offers

If your listing receives multiple offers at the same time, review them in chronological order. Once you accept one offer, mark the listing as pending or communicate with other interested buyers to avoid confusion. Do not accept multiple offers on the same item.

Counter-Offer Strategy

A buyer who makes an offer is genuinely interested. If their offer is within reasonable range (say, 10–25% below your price), a counter-offer meeting them halfway often closes the deal. Being flexible without underselling yourself is the sweet spot of successful negotiating.

Tip: A friendly, quick response to offers — even to say you're declining — builds a positive reputation. Buyers remember sellers who communicate well and are more likely to return or leave good reviews.
Warning: Accepting an offer is a commitment to complete the transaction. Only accept when you are fully ready to proceed with delivery. Repeatedly backing out of accepted offers can result in account penalties.

faqs.q: How long do I have to respond to an offer?
faqs.a: Typically 24–48 hours before the offer expires. Respond promptly — slow responses lose sales.

faqs.q: Can I accept more than one offer on the same listing?
faqs.a: No. Once you accept one offer, the listing should be marked as pending or sold. You can only sell the item once.

faqs.q: What if I accidentally accept an offer I didn't mean to?
faqs.a: Contact the buyer immediately via messages to explain the situation and reach out to Swapedly support for assistance.

faqs.q: Can I send a counter-offer to every incoming offer?
faqs.a: Yes. Counter-offers are always an option. Just keep counters reasonable — multiple rounds of back-and-forth can frustrate buyers.

faqs.q: What if the buyer accepts my counter-offer but then disappears?
faqs.a: Open a dispute or report the issue to Swapedly support. Buyers who repeatedly agree to deals and don't follow through may face account consequences.

articles.title: Highlighting Your Listings
articles.summary: A listing highlight boosts your item's visibility in search results and the marketplace feed, helping it sell faster.
articles.content:
What Is a Listing Highlight?

A listing highlight is a paid visibility boost that elevates your listing above standard results in the marketplace. Highlighted listings receive a prominent placement in search results and the browsing feed, along with a visual badge that distinguishes them from non-highlighted listings. If you have an item that isn't getting the attention it deserves, a highlight can make a significant difference.

What Highlighting Your Listing Does

  • Increased placement — Your listing appears higher in search results and marketplace browsing feeds, so more buyers see it.
  • Visual badge — A highlighted indicator on your listing catches the eye of browsers who might otherwise scroll past.
  • More impressions — More views translates directly to more offers and faster sales, especially for popular item categories.

How to Add a Highlight

  1. Go to My Listings from the main navigation.
  2. Find the listing you want to boost.
  3. Tap the "Highlight" option on the listing card or inside the listing edit screen.
  4. Confirm the highlight — it activates immediately and the boost starts working right away.

Swapedly Plus and Free Highlights

Swapedly Plus members receive 5 free listing highlights every month as part of their $9.99/month subscription. These refresh at the start of each billing cycle. For active sellers, the 5 monthly highlights alone can be worth more than the subscription cost in additional sales velocity.

Non-Plus members may be able to purchase highlights individually at a per-use rate. Check the listing options screen for current pricing and availability in your market.

Tip: Use highlights strategically. Apply them to your highest-value listings, items that have been sitting for a while without traction, or listings you urgently need to move. Don't waste a highlight on a listing with weak photos or an uncompetitive price — fix those issues first, then highlight.
Warning: Monthly highlight allocations from Swapedly Plus typically do not carry over to the next billing cycle. Use your 5 highlights before your billing date resets or they'll expire unused.

faqs.q: How many highlights does a Swapedly Plus subscription include?
faqs.a: Swapedly Plus includes 5 highlights per month, which refresh at the start of each new billing cycle.

faqs.q: Do unused highlights roll over to next month?
faqs.a: No. Monthly highlights from Plus do not carry over. Use them before your billing cycle resets.

faqs.q: How long does a highlight last once applied?
faqs.a: Highlight duration varies. Check the listing options screen for current duration terms when applying.

faqs.q: Will highlighting guarantee that my item sells?
faqs.a: Highlights increase visibility and views, but a sale still depends on competitive pricing, quality photos, and buyer demand. Fix any listing weaknesses before highlighting.

faqs.q: Can I remove a highlight once it's applied?
faqs.a: Highlights are typically time-limited and expire automatically. They generally cannot be manually removed once applied.

title: Swap Bucks
description: Everything about Swap Bucks — Swapedly's virtual currency — including how to earn, spend, redeem, and manage them.

articles.title: What Are Swap Bucks?
articles.summary: Swap Bucks (SB) are Swapedly's virtual currency — used to buy and sell items with a simple 1 SB = $1 USD value.
articles.content:
The Currency That Powers Swapedly

Swap Bucks (SB) are the virtual currency at the heart of the Swapedly marketplace. Every item is priced in SB, every sale pays out in SB, and the balance you accumulate is what you spend when you buy something new. The key rule to remember: 1 SB = $1 USD in value. It's a straightforward, transparent system.

Why Swap Bucks Instead of Cash?

The SB system creates a self-sustaining community economy. When you sell an item, you don't cash out and leave — you earn SB that you spend on other items from fellow community members. This circular flow keeps value inside the Swapedly ecosystem, encourages active participation, and makes the platform feel more like a true swap community than a standard resale site.

Key Facts About Swap Bucks

  • Exchange rate: 1 SB = $1 USD in value
  • How you earn SB: Selling items, referring friends (1 SB per sign-up), claiming the welcome gift card (40 SB), and periodic platform bonuses
  • How you spend SB: Purchasing items from other sellers in the marketplace
  • Marketplace unlock threshold: You need at least 60 SB in your wallet to access the marketplace and buy items
  • Welcome gift: New users can claim 40 SB at swapedly.com/#/gift-card
  • Escrow: SB are held securely during transactions and released only after the buyer confirms delivery

Swap Bucks vs. Purchase Credits

SB cover item prices. Purchase Credits are a separate, USD-denominated currency used only to pay the 10% transaction fee on purchases and sales. You'll need both to participate fully on Swapedly.

Are Swap Bucks Refundable?

SB earned through sales or bonuses are platform credits and are not redeemable for cash. However, if a transaction dispute is resolved in your favor as a buyer, your escrowed SB are returned to your wallet. See What to Do If Something Goes Wrong for the dispute process.

Tip: Think of your SB balance as a shopping budget earned through your own selling activity. The more you sell, the more you can buy — and with Swapedly Plus's 1.5x boost, that virtuous cycle works even faster. See Ways to Earn Swap Bucks.

faqs.q: Can I convert Swap Bucks into real money?
faqs.a: No. Swap Bucks are a platform-specific virtual currency. They cannot be redeemed for cash, gift cards, or any other real-world value outside of Swapedly.

faqs.q: Do Swap Bucks expire?
faqs.a: Once credited to your wallet, Swap Bucks generally do not expire. Check the platform terms for any specific conditions.

faqs.q: Can I transfer Swap Bucks to another user?
faqs.a: SB can only move between users as part of a legitimate item transaction. Direct peer-to-peer SB transfers are not supported.

faqs.q: Where do I check my Swap Bucks balance?
faqs.a: Your balance is shown in the Wallet section, accessible from the main navigation menu.

faqs.q: What happens to my SB during a transaction?
faqs.a: When you buy, your SB move into escrow — held securely by Swapedly — until you confirm delivery. Then they're released to the seller.

articles.title: How to Redeem a Gift Card for Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Redeem a Swapedly gift card to instantly add Swap Bucks to your wallet — including the $40 new-user welcome gift.
articles.content:
Add Swap Bucks Instantly with a Gift Card

Gift cards are one of the fastest ways to add Swap Bucks to your wallet. Whether you're a new user claiming the $40 welcome bonus or someone who received a Swapedly gift card from a friend, the redemption process is simple and takes less than a minute from start to finish.

How to Redeem Your Gift Card

  1. Make sure you're logged in — Gift cards are redeemed into your account, so you need to be signed in before starting. If you don't have an account yet, see How to Create an Account first.
  2. Navigate to the gift card page — Go to swapedly.com/#/gift-card. You can find this link in your welcome email, on the Swapedly homepage, or during the welcome tour.
  3. Enter your gift card code (if applicable) — If your card has a unique redemption code (e.g., a gift card given to you by another person), type it into the redemption field exactly as shown. For the new-user welcome bonus, the page will recognize your eligible account automatically — no code needed.
  4. Tap "Redeem" — Confirm the redemption on screen.
  5. Check your wallet — The Swap Bucks are credited to your wallet immediately. Navigate to Understanding Your Wallet to confirm they've appeared.

The New User Welcome Gift ($40 = 40 SB)

Every new Swapedly account is eligible for a one-time welcome gift of 40 Swap Bucks — equivalent to $40 in marketplace value. This gets you most of the way to the 60 SB minimum required to unlock the marketplace. You only need 20 more SB to start buying.

Here's how to close that gap fast:

  • Refer 20 friends — 1 SB per sign-up. See Referring Friends for Swap Bucks.
  • List and sell one item — Even a small sale puts you over the threshold.
  • Complete your profile — Bonus SB may be available for finishing your profile setup.
Tip: Claim your welcome gift card on the same day you sign up. Don't leave 40 free SB sitting unclaimed while you explore the platform.
Warning: Only redeem gift cards through the official Swapedly page at swapedly.com/#/gift-card. Be skeptical of any third-party website or message claiming to offer Swapedly gift codes at a discount — these are almost always scams.

faqs.q: Where do I redeem my Swapedly gift card?
faqs.a: Visit swapedly.com/#/gift-card while logged into your account and follow the on-screen steps.

faqs.q: Can I redeem more than one gift card?
faqs.a: The new-user welcome gift can only be claimed once per account. Additional gift cards with unique codes can be redeemed separately as long as each code is valid.

faqs.q: My gift card code isn't working — what should I do?
faqs.a: Double-check that you've entered the code exactly as shown, including uppercase letters. If it still fails, contact Swapedly support with the code details.

faqs.q: How quickly do the Swap Bucks appear in my wallet?
faqs.a: Gift card SB are credited to your wallet instantly upon successful redemption.

faqs.q: Can I give a Swapedly gift card to someone else?
faqs.a: Unique gift card codes can be shared with others for redemption into their own accounts. The new-user welcome bonus is account-specific and cannot be transferred or used by another person.

articles.title: Ways to Earn Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Discover every way to earn Swap Bucks on Swapedly — from selling items and referring friends to claiming bonuses.
articles.content:
Building Your SB Balance

There are multiple paths to earning Swap Bucks on Swapedly, and you don't need to sell a single item to get started. Whether you're a buyer trying to hit the 60 SB marketplace threshold or an active seller looking to maximize your earnings, here's every earning opportunity available to you.

Primary Earning Methods

  • Sell items — The most powerful way to earn SB. When a buyer purchases your listing and confirms delivery, the agreed SB price (minus nothing — the fee is separate in USD) is released from escrow into your wallet. List anything you no longer need and turn it into buying power.
  • Welcome gift card (new users only) — Claim 40 SB instantly at swapedly.com/#/gift-card. This is available once per account to all new users and requires no purchase. See How to Redeem a Gift Card.
  • Refer friends — Earn 1 SB for every friend who signs up using your unique referral link. There's no stated cap, so share widely. Twenty referrals gets you 20 SB, closing the gap to marketplace access from the welcome gift alone. See Referring Friends for Swap Bucks.
  • Promotional and seasonal bonuses — Swapedly periodically offers bonus SB for completing specific actions (finishing your profile, hitting a sales milestone, participating in a promotion). Keep notifications on so you don't miss these opportunities.

Swapedly Plus: The 1.5x Sales Boost

Swapedly Plus subscribers earn 1.5x Swap Bucks on every sale. That means a 40 SB sale becomes 60 SB in your wallet — a 50% bonus on top of your normal earnings. If you sell regularly, the $9.99/month subscription can easily pay for itself within a few transactions. See How the 1.5x Swap Bucks Boost Works.

How Fast Can I Unlock the Marketplace?

You need 60 SB to unlock marketplace access. With the 40 SB welcome gift, you're just 20 SB short. Here are your options:

  • Refer 20 friends → 20 SB (instant, no selling required)
  • List one item and sell it for 20+ SB
  • Combine referrals and profile bonuses
Tip: If you're new and want to buy before selling anything, the welcome gift card plus referrals is the fastest path to 60 SB with zero investment.

faqs.q: Can I earn Swap Bucks without listing or selling anything?
faqs.a: Yes. The welcome gift card (40 SB) and referral bonuses (1 SB per sign-up) let you earn SB without listing a single item.

faqs.q: How many SB do I earn per referral?
faqs.a: You earn 1 Swap Buck for each friend who creates a Swapedly account using your unique referral link.

faqs.q: When do I receive SB from a completed sale?
faqs.a: SB are held in escrow during the transaction and released to your wallet once the buyer confirms delivery.

faqs.q: Are there limits on how many referrals I can make?
faqs.a: There's no stated maximum for referral earnings. Refer as many friends as you like.

faqs.q: Does Swapedly Plus increase SB from referrals and bonuses, or just sales?
faqs.a: The 1.5x boost applies specifically to SB earned from selling items. Referral and bonus SB are credited at the standard rate.

articles.title: How to Spend Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Use your Swap Bucks to purchase items from other sellers in the Swapedly marketplace — here's exactly how it works.
articles.content:
Putting Your SB to Work

Once you've accumulated Swap Bucks, spending them is the rewarding half of the Swapedly experience. You browse the marketplace for items you want, pay with your SB balance, and wait for delivery. Here's a complete walkthrough of how spending works — and what to keep in mind to avoid common pitfalls.

The Main Way to Spend SB: Buying Items

The marketplace is where your Swap Bucks become real-world goods. Find an item you want, confirm the purchase, and your SB are committed. Here's the flow:

  1. Browse the marketplace — Search, filter, and find something you want. Requires 60 SB in your wallet to access. See Searching and Filtering Listings.
  2. Review the listing carefully — Check the description, photos, condition, price, and seller profile before committing.
  3. Tap "Buy Now" or accept an offer — Your SB are immediately moved into escrow, secured by Swapedly.
  4. Receive and inspect the item — Once it arrives, check everything against the listing.
  5. Confirm delivery — If satisfied, confirm in the app to release the seller's SB. See Confirming Delivery and Releasing Swap Bucks.

Don't Drop Below 60 SB

The marketplace requires a minimum balance of 60 SB in your wallet at all times to maintain access. If a purchase drops you below that threshold, you may temporarily lose the ability to browse and buy until your balance recovers through a new sale or referral bonus.

Tip: Maintain a buffer above 60 SB so a single purchase doesn't lock you out of the marketplace. Sell an item before buying a more expensive one to keep your balance healthy.

Remember: Two Currencies at Play

Item prices are paid in SB, but the 10% transaction fee (capped at $20) is paid separately in USD via Purchase Credits. Make sure you have both ready before completing a purchase — you'll need enough SB for the item price and enough Purchase Credits for the fee.

Warning: Only spend Swap Bucks through Swapedly's official purchase flow. Never send SB to another user outside of a formal transaction — off-platform transfers are not protected and may violate platform rules.

faqs.q: Can I spend SB on anything outside of item purchases?
faqs.a: Currently, Swap Bucks are used exclusively to purchase items listed by other sellers in the Swapedly marketplace.

faqs.q: What if I don't have enough SB to buy an item I want?
faqs.a: You'll need to earn more SB through sales, referrals, or bonuses before completing that purchase.

faqs.q: Will buying something drop my balance below the 60 SB marketplace minimum?
faqs.a: Yes, it's possible. If your balance falls below 60 SB, you may temporarily lose marketplace access until you earn more SB.

faqs.q: Can I split a purchase — paying part in SB and part in Purchase Credits?
faqs.a: No. Item prices are paid fully in SB. Purchase Credits are only used for the transaction fee — they cannot supplement your SB balance for item costs.

faqs.q: Is there a maximum amount I can spend per transaction?
faqs.a: There's no stated spending cap, but the transaction fee is capped at $20 regardless of the item price.

articles.title: Understanding Your Wallet
articles.summary: Your Swapedly wallet is your financial hub — tracking your Swap Bucks balance, escrowed SB, Purchase Credits, and full transaction history.
articles.content:
Your Financial Dashboard

The Wallet section of your Swapedly account is the central place for all your financial activity on the platform. Whether you're checking what you've earned from a recent sale, buying more Purchase Credits, or reviewing your transaction history, everything lives here.

What You'll Find in Your Wallet

  • Available Swap Bucks balance — Your current SB that are free to spend on purchases. This is what counts toward the 60 SB marketplace access threshold.
  • Escrowed Swap Bucks — SB that are tied up in active, in-progress transactions. These are committed but not yet finalized — they'll either be released to a seller when you confirm delivery, or returned to you if a transaction is cancelled or resolved in your favor via dispute. Escrowed SB do not count toward your available balance.
  • Purchase Credits balance — Your remaining USD-denominated credits for paying transaction fees. Displayed separately from SB.
  • Transaction history — A complete, chronological log of every SB movement in and out of your wallet: sales, purchases, referral bonuses, gift card redemptions, escrow holds, fee payments, and dispute resolutions.

How to Find Your Wallet

  1. Log in to Swapedly.
  2. Tap Wallet in the main navigation menu, or tap your balance display if it's shown in the header.
  3. Your balances and history load immediately.

Reading Your Transaction History

Each line item in your history shows the date, transaction type (sale, purchase, referral, fee, gift card, escrow release, etc.), the SB or credit amount, and your running balance after that transaction. This log is invaluable for tracking your overall activity and identifying any discrepancies.

Topping Up Purchase Credits

If your Purchase Credits are running low, tap "Buy Credits" within the wallet section. Each credit costs $0.49 with a minimum purchase of $5.00. See What Are Purchase Credits? for full details.

Tip: Review your wallet after every transaction to confirm SB were credited or debited correctly. Catching discrepancies early makes them much easier to resolve through support.

faqs.q: Why is my available SB balance lower than I expected?
faqs.a: Check your transaction history for recent activity. SB tied up in active transactions appear as "escrowed" and aren't part of your available balance until the transaction completes.

faqs.q: Can I see a history of all my referral bonuses?
faqs.a: Yes. Referral bonuses appear as individual line items in your transaction history with a "referral" label.

faqs.q: When does escrowed SB become available again?
faqs.a: Escrowed SB are released once the buyer confirms delivery (moving to the seller's wallet) or when a transaction is cancelled or resolved in the buyer's favor.

faqs.q: Can I export or download my transaction history?
faqs.a: Check wallet settings for an export option. If unavailable, contact Swapedly support to request a history report.

faqs.q: What's the difference between my total SB and my available SB?
faqs.a: Available SB is what you can spend right now. Total SB includes both available and escrowed amounts. You can only spend available SB.

articles.title: Referring Friends for Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Earn 1 Swap Buck for every friend who signs up via your referral link — one of the easiest ways to build your SB balance.
articles.content:
Share Swapedly. Earn SB.

The Swapedly referral program rewards you for growing the community. Every time a friend signs up for Swapedly using your unique referral link, you earn 1 Swap Buck — automatically, no further action required.
