It might sound small, but it adds up: twenty referrals fills the 20 SB gap between your welcome gift (40 SB) and marketplace access (60 SB minimum), getting you buying access with zero selling required.

How to Get Your Referral Link

  1. Log in to your Swapedly account.
  2. Navigate to your profile or look for a "Refer a Friend" section in the main menu or settings.
  3. Copy your unique referral link — This link is specific to your account and is what triggers the credit when a new user signs up through it.
  4. Share it — Send it via text, post it on social media, include it in an email, add it to your bio. The more places it lives, the more passive sign-ups you'll generate.

How the Referral Credit Works

  1. Your friend clicks your link and lands on the Swapedly sign-up page.
  2. They create and verify their account.
  3. Your account is automatically credited with 1 SB.
  4. The credit appears in your wallet transaction history with a "referral" label.

Maximizing Your Referrals

  • Mention the $40 welcome gift card — It's a strong incentive. New users get 40 free SB just for signing up — that's essentially free money in the marketplace.
  • Share in relevant communities — Facebook Marketplace groups, local buy/sell groups, and thrift or resale communities are great audiences.
  • Add your link to social media bios — Passive placement means referrals can trickle in over time without repeated effort.
Tip: Track your referral earnings in your wallet transaction history to see how many sign-ups your link has generated and how your balance is growing.
Warning: Creating fake accounts or exploiting the referral system in any way (including referring yourself through alternate accounts) violates Swapedly's terms. Fraudulently earned referral SB will be reversed and your account may be suspended.

faqs.q: How much do I earn per referral?
faqs.a: You earn exactly 1 Swap Buck (1 SB = $1 USD in value) for each friend who creates a Swapedly account using your unique referral link.

faqs.q: Is there a limit on how many friends I can refer?
faqs.a: There's no stated limit. Refer as many people as you like within the rules of the program.

faqs.q: When do my referral SB appear in my wallet?
faqs.a: Referral SB are typically credited automatically once the referred person completes account sign-up and verification.

faqs.q: My friend signed up but I didn't receive my SB — what do I do?
faqs.a: Make sure your friend used your exact referral link, not just the homepage URL. If the credit is still missing after a day, contact Swapedly support with details.

faqs.q: Does my referred friend get any bonus for signing up through my link?
faqs.a: Your friend still gets the standard new-user welcome offer, including the $40 gift card at swapedly.com/#/gift-card. Your referral link doesn't reduce their benefits.

articles.title: Unlocking the Marketplace (60 SB Required)
articles.summary: You need 60 Swap Bucks in your wallet to unlock the Swapedly marketplace — here's why it exists and the fastest ways to get there.
articles.content:
Why Is the Marketplace Locked?

New Swapedly accounts need a minimum of 60 Swap Bucks in their wallet before they can browse and purchase items in the marketplace. This threshold exists for good reasons: it ensures that marketplace participants are genuinely engaged with the platform's SB economy, deters fraudulent and throwaway accounts from flooding the marketplace, and helps maintain the quality and trustworthiness of the buying pool.

Where You Stand After the Welcome Gift

If you've claimed your welcome gift card, you already have 40 SB — meaning you're just 20 SB short of marketplace access. Here are the fastest ways to close that gap:

  • Refer 20 friends — 1 SB per sign-up, no selling required. Share your link on social media, in a text chain, or anywhere else. See Referring Friends for Swap Bucks.
  • List and sell one item — Clear something out of your home for 20+ SB and you'll unlock access immediately upon delivery confirmation.
  • Complete your profile for bonus SB — Swapedly may offer SB incentives for completing profile setup. Check your notifications for active offers.
  • Watch for promotional bonuses — Swapedly runs limited-time SB promotions. Keep your notifications active so you don't miss them.

Important: 60 SB Is a Threshold, Not a Fee

The 60 SB minimum is a wallet balance requirement, not a charge. Your SB are not deducted to unlock access — they simply need to be present in your wallet. You keep all 60 SB and can spend them freely on purchases.

Tip: Once you unlock the marketplace, maintain a healthy SB buffer. If a purchase drops you below 60 SB, marketplace access is temporarily suspended until your balance recovers through a new sale or referral.

Can't Claim the Gift Card?

If you're having trouble with the gift card redemption, see How to Redeem a Gift Card for Swap Bucks or contact support directly.

Warning: If your balance drops below 60 SB after a purchase, you'll temporarily lose marketplace browsing and buying access. Sell an item or earn referral SB to restore it.

faqs.q: Is the 60 SB a fee, or just a minimum balance requirement?
faqs.a: It's a minimum balance threshold — not a fee. Your SB stay in your wallet; you just need 60 SB present to have marketplace access.

faqs.q: What happens if my balance drops below 60 SB?
faqs.a: Marketplace browsing and buying access is temporarily suspended until your balance returns to 60 SB or above.

faqs.q: I have the 40 SB from the gift card but still can't access the marketplace. Why?
faqs.a: You need 60 SB total — the gift card gets you to 40. Earn the remaining 20 through referrals, a sale, or available bonuses.

faqs.q: Can I unlock the marketplace without selling or referring anyone?
faqs.a: If there are no other bonus opportunities available, selling or referring friends are the two guaranteed paths. The welcome gift gets you most of the way there.

faqs.q: Does the 60 SB threshold ever change?
faqs.a: The 60 SB requirement is the current platform standard. Check Swapedly's announcements for any future changes to this policy.

title: Shipping & Pickup
description: Everything about getting items from sellers to buyers — whether by mail or in-person local pickup.

articles.title: Shipping vs. Local Pickup — Which Should I Choose?
articles.summary: Compare shipping and local pickup to understand which delivery method makes the most sense for your transaction.
articles.content:
Two Ways to Exchange Items on Swapedly

When a seller creates a listing, they choose the delivery method: shipping, local pickup, or both. As a buyer, you work within whatever the seller has made available. Understanding the pros and cons of each method helps you set expectations and choose listings that work for your situation.

Shipping: Buy and Sell from Anywhere

With shipping, the seller packages the item and sends it via a carrier (USPS, UPS, FedEx, etc.). Neither party needs to be in the same location.

  • Pros: No geographic restriction — buy from anyone in the country. Convenient — no need to coordinate schedules or meet in person. Suitable for small items that pack easily.
  • Cons: Shipping costs add to the total (check whether the seller includes shipping in the SB price or charges separately). Delivery takes time. Items can be damaged in transit if not packed well. You can't physically inspect the item before it arrives.
  • Best for: Small-to-medium items, buyers and sellers who aren't near each other, or when you prefer not to meet in person.

Local Pickup: Fast, Inspectable, No Shipping Cost

Local pickup means you and the seller meet in person to complete the exchange. The Swapedly transaction still happens through the platform (your SB move into escrow when you purchase), but you physically collect the item at the agreed location.

  • Pros: No shipping cost. You inspect the item in person before confirming delivery. Faster — often same-day or next-day. Great for large or fragile items that would be risky to ship.
  • Cons: Requires both parties to be in the same geographic area. Scheduling and coordinating a meetup takes some back-and-forth. Requires basic safety precautions for an in-person exchange.
  • Best for: Furniture, large appliances, and other items that can't be shipped economically. Also ideal when you want to inspect the item hands-on before committing.

Who Decides the Delivery Method?

The seller chooses the delivery method(s) when creating their listing. As a buyer, you'll see what's available on the listing page. If only one method is offered and it doesn't work for you, message the seller — they may be willing to accommodate.

Tip: For local pickup, always meet in a public place. See Tips for Safe Local Meetups for a full safety guide.
Warning: Regardless of the delivery method, always complete the transaction through Swapedly. Paying in cash or via any external method voids your transaction protection entirely.

faqs.q: Can a listing offer both shipping and local pickup?
faqs.a: Yes. Sellers can enable both options, giving buyers the flexibility to choose what works best for them.

faqs.q: Who pays for shipping — buyer or seller?
faqs.a: This varies by listing. Check the listing description to see whether shipping is included in the SB price or charged as an additional amount.

faqs.q: What if I want local pickup but the seller only offers shipping?
faqs.a: Message the seller and ask if they'd consider local pickup. It's their call, but many sellers are open to it if you're nearby.

faqs.q: Is local pickup safe?
faqs.a: Local pickup is very safe when you take basic precautions — always meet in a public place during daylight. See our Safe Local Meetup guide.

faqs.q: Does Swapedly's transaction protection cover both delivery methods?
faqs.a: Yes. Both shipping and local pickup transactions are fully covered by escrow protection, as long as the purchase is made through Swapedly.

articles.title: How Shipping Works on Swapedly
articles.summary: A step-by-step guide to the shipping process on Swapedly — what both buyers and sellers need to do from purchase to delivery.
articles.content:
Shipping from Start to Finish

Shipped transactions on Swapedly follow a clear sequence that protects both parties. Here's the complete process from the moment a buyer clicks "Buy Now" to the moment the seller receives their Swap Bucks.

For Sellers: What You Need to Do

  1. Receive the purchase notification — Once a buyer completes a purchase, you'll be notified immediately. The buyer's SB are now in escrow.
  2. Confirm the shipping address — Message the buyer through Swapedly to confirm their delivery address before packaging anything. Addresses change, and it's worth double-checking.
  3. Package the item carefully — Use appropriate materials to prevent damage in transit. See Packaging Tips for Safe Shipping for detailed guidance.
  4. Ship the item promptly — Use a carrier that provides tracking: USPS, UPS, FedEx, or similar. Tracking is strongly recommended — without it, proving delivery in a dispute is nearly impossible.
  5. Share the tracking number — Send the tracking number to the buyer via Swapedly messages as soon as you have it. Buyers appreciate being able to track their package.
  6. Wait for delivery confirmation — Once the buyer receives and confirms the item, your Swap Bucks are released from escrow into your wallet. You'll receive a notification when this happens.

For Buyers: What You Need to Do

  1. Check your messages — The seller may reach out to confirm your shipping address. Respond promptly.
  2. Track your package — Use the tracking number the seller provides to monitor your shipment's progress.
  3. Be available for delivery — If a signature is required, make sure someone is available or check with the carrier about alternative delivery options.
  4. Inspect on arrival — Open and check the item against the listing description and photos before confirming delivery.
  5. Confirm delivery in the app — If everything is as expected, confirm delivery to release the seller's SB. See Confirming Delivery and Releasing Swap Bucks.
Tip: Sellers — ship within 1–3 business days of a sale. Slow shipping is a common source of negative reviews. A fast, communicative seller builds a reputation that leads to more future sales.
Warning: Buyers — never confirm delivery before you have physically received and inspected the item. Confirming early releases the seller's SB and closes the dispute window.

faqs.q: Who is responsible for paying shipping costs?
faqs.a: Check the individual listing. Some sellers include shipping in the SB price; others charge separately. The listing description should clarify.

faqs.q: What if my item arrives damaged from shipping?
faqs.a: Photograph the damage immediately and open a dispute through Swapedly before confirming delivery. Do not discard the original packaging.

faqs.q: How long should I wait for an item to arrive?
faqs.a: Domestic shipping typically takes 3–10 business days. Check the tracking number provided by the seller for the most accurate estimate.

faqs.q: What if the seller never provides a tracking number?
faqs.a: Message the seller to request it. If they don't respond or the item never arrives, open a dispute. Don't confirm delivery without receiving the item.

faqs.q: Can I change my shipping address after completing a purchase?
faqs.a: Contact the seller via messages immediately if you need to change the address. If the item hasn't shipped yet, they may be able to update it.

articles.title: The Local Pickup Process
articles.summary: How to coordinate and safely complete a local pickup on Swapedly — from purchase to in-person exchange.
articles.content:
How Local Pickup Works on Swapedly

Local pickup lets buyers and sellers complete a transaction by meeting in person, rather than shipping. It eliminates shipping costs and lets the buyer inspect the item hands-on before finalizing the deal. Even though the exchange is in-person, the transaction itself still runs through Swapedly to keep everyone protected.

Step-by-Step: The Local Pickup Process

  1. Buyer completes the purchase — The buyer taps "Buy Now" in the app. Their Swap Bucks immediately move into escrow — secured by Swapedly before anyone meets up.
  2. Coordinate the meetup — Buyer and seller communicate via Swapedly messages to agree on a time and location. Always choose a public, populated place. See Tips for Safe Local Meetups.
  3. Meet at the agreed location — Show up at the arranged time. Both parties should arrive with the Swapedly app accessible on their phone.
  4. Seller presents the item — The seller brings the item in the condition described. The buyer inspects it thoroughly: check for described flaws, test functionality if applicable, and compare against the listing photos.
  5. Buyer confirms in the app — If the item meets expectations, the buyer opens the Swapedly app and confirms delivery right there at the meetup. This is important: don't wait until you get home to confirm.
  6. SB released to the seller — The moment the buyer confirms, Swapedly releases the escrowed Swap Bucks to the seller's wallet. Both parties receive confirmation.

If Something Isn't Right at the Meetup

If the item doesn't match the listing description — different condition, missing parts, or other significant discrepancy — the buyer should not confirm delivery. Walk away, then open a dispute through the Swapedly app. The escrowed SB will remain frozen until the dispute is resolved.

Tip: Confirm delivery in the app while you're still at the meetup location, not hours later from home. This creates a clean, time-stamped record of the transaction and resolves it properly for both parties.
Warning: Never exchange cash or pay via any outside method during a local pickup. The entire transaction must go through Swapedly to maintain escrow protection for both parties.

faqs.q: Can I inspect the item before confirming delivery?
faqs.a: Absolutely — and you should. Inspect the item thoroughly at the meetup before tapping confirm in the app. That's the entire point of local pickup.

faqs.q: What if the item is significantly not as described when I see it in person?
faqs.a: Do not confirm delivery. Leave the meetup and open a dispute through the Swapedly app. The escrowed SB will not be released to the seller until the dispute is resolved.

faqs.q: Is there a time limit for completing a local pickup after purchase?
faqs.a: Try to schedule and complete the pickup within a few days. Prolonged delays can create complications and may be flagged by the platform.

faqs.q: Can I bring a friend to the local pickup?
faqs.a: Yes, and it's encouraged — especially for higher-value items or if you're meeting someone you don't know. Having company improves safety.

faqs.q: What if the seller doesn't show up to the agreed meetup?
faqs.a: Don't confirm delivery. Open a dispute and report the issue to Swapedly support. The seller's no-show will be taken into account.

articles.title: Packaging Tips for Safe Shipping
articles.summary: Pack your items correctly to prevent damage in transit and ensure your buyer receives exactly what was listed.
articles.content:
Why Packaging Is a Seller Responsibility

When you ship an item, you're responsible for getting it to the buyer in the condition you described. Shipping carriers are not gentle — packages are stacked, dropped, and sorted by machines. A well-packed item survives the journey; a poorly packed one often doesn't. Damaged items are the most common trigger for disputes and negative seller reviews. Taking five extra minutes to pack correctly is always worth it.

General Packaging Guidelines

  • Choose the right box — Use a sturdy cardboard box appropriate for the item's size and weight. Double-wall boxes are best for heavy or fragile items. The item should fit with room for padding but not so much space that it shifts around excessively.
  • Add generous padding — Bubble wrap, packing peanuts, crumpled newspaper, foam inserts, or even clean clothing all work as padding. The item should not be able to move or shift inside the box when you shake it.
  • Wrap components separately — If the item has multiple parts (e.g., a camera with lenses, a console with controllers), wrap each component individually before placing them together in the box.
  • Reinforce the box — Use quality packing tape — not masking tape or duct tape — to seal all seams. Apply tape in an H-pattern across the top and bottom flaps. Reinforce the bottom of any heavy box with extra strips of tape.
  • Label clearly — Print or write the shipping address legibly on the outside of the box. Include your return address. Keep a copy of the label information in case the package needs to be traced.

Shipping Fragile Items

For anything fragile — glassware, ceramics, electronics with screens, vintage items — use the double-box method: place the item in a smaller inner box with its own padding, then place that box inside a larger outer box with additional padding around it. This is the gold standard for fragile shipments and significantly reduces breakage rates.

Document Before You Seal

Take a clear photo of the item inside the packaging before you seal the box. If a dispute arises about damage in transit, this photo proves the item was properly packaged and in good condition when it left your hands.

Tip: For high-value items, purchase shipping insurance from your carrier. It typically costs a few dollars and provides coverage if the package is lost or damaged in transit — worth it for anything over $50.

faqs.q: Can I reuse old shipping boxes?
faqs.a: Yes, as long as the box is structurally sound — no crushed corners, tears, or significant damage. Remove or black out any old labels and bar codes to avoid misdirection.

faqs.q: What if I don't have bubble wrap or packing peanuts?
faqs.a: Crumpled newspaper, clean clothing, towels, or foam from product packaging all work as padding alternatives. The key is that the item can't move around inside the box.

faqs.q: Should I add shipping insurance for valuable items?
faqs.a: Yes, strongly recommended for higher-value items. Shipping insurance covers you if the package is lost or arrives damaged and provides recourse with the carrier.

faqs.q: Do I need to include a packing slip inside the box?
faqs.a: Not required, but a brief handwritten note or printed packing slip inside the box is a nice touch that buyers genuinely appreciate and can help with any package mix-up.

faqs.q: What if my item is too large or awkward to ship conventionally?
faqs.a: For oversized items, explore freight shipping options or — better yet — set your listing to local pickup only when you create it.

articles.title: Confirming Delivery and Releasing Swap Bucks
articles.summary: Confirming delivery is the final step of every Swapedly transaction — it releases the seller's escrowed Swap Bucks once you're satisfied.
articles.content:
The Final Step That Completes a Transaction

Every Swapedly transaction ends with the buyer confirming delivery. This single action signals to the platform that the exchange went as expected, releasing the seller's Swap Bucks from escrow into their wallet. Until you confirm, the SB remain frozen — protected for you in case something went wrong.

How to Confirm Delivery

  1. Receive the item — Wait until you physically have the item in hand, whether it arrived by shipping or you picked it up in person.
  2. Inspect it carefully — Compare the item to the listing photos and description. Check the condition, functionality, and anything specifically mentioned in the listing. Look at all sides.
  3. Open the transaction in Swapedly — Go to your purchases or active transactions list in the app.
  4. Tap "Confirm Delivery" — This button appears within the transaction details. Tapping it signals that you received the item as described and are satisfied with the exchange.
  5. Leave a review (optional but encouraged) — After confirming, you'll have the opportunity to rate the seller and leave written feedback. Reviews help other buyers make informed decisions and reward good sellers.

Confirming for Local Pickup

For local pickups, confirm delivery in the app while you're still at the meetup location — right after you've inspected the item and are satisfied. This creates a clean, timestamped record for both parties.

What If Something Is Wrong?

If the item doesn't match the listing description, arrives damaged, or is otherwise not what you paid for, do not confirm delivery. Open a dispute through the platform immediately. See What to Do If Something Goes Wrong for the full process. Your escrowed SB remain protected until the dispute is resolved.

Tip: Confirm delivery promptly once you're satisfied. Sellers depend on timely SB release to buy more items and continue selling. A quick confirmation builds goodwill and earns you good seller reciprocity over time.
Warning: Confirming delivery is final. Once you confirm, the SB are transferred to the seller and the transaction is marked complete. It becomes significantly harder to resolve disputes after confirmation — only confirm when you are genuinely satisfied with the item.

faqs.q: Do I have to confirm delivery immediately when the item arrives?
faqs.a: Inspect the item first, then confirm. There's a deadline — check the transaction details for your specific window — so don't leave it too long.

faqs.q: What happens if I forget to confirm delivery?
faqs.a: After a set period past the expected delivery date, Swapedly may automatically release the SB to the seller. Confirm promptly to control the timing yourself.

faqs.q: Can I undo a delivery confirmation?
faqs.a: No. Confirmation is final. Only confirm when you're fully satisfied. If you confirm and then discover an issue, contact support immediately.

faqs.q: What if I confirmed delivery but the item turned out to be broken?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support right away. Resolution after confirmation is harder but the team will still review the situation, especially with supporting evidence like photos.

faqs.q: Does confirming delivery also submit a rating for the seller?
faqs.a: After confirming, you're given the option to leave a review. It's optional but strongly encouraged — it helps the entire community.

title: Account & Settings
description: Manage every aspect of your Swapedly account — password, email, profile, notifications, and account closure.

articles.title: Resetting Your Password
articles.summary: Forgot your Swapedly password? Reset it in a few steps and get back into your account quickly.
articles.content:
Locked Out? Here's How to Get Back In

Forgetting a password happens to everyone. Swapedly makes it easy to reset yours and regain access to your account in just a couple of minutes. The process is secure, straightforward, and entirely self-service — no need to contact support unless you've also lost access to your email.

How to Reset Your Password

  1. Go to the forgot password page — Visit swapedly.com/#/forgot-password directly, or click "Forgot Password?" on the Swapedly login screen.
  2. Enter your account email address — Type the email address associated with your Swapedly account and tap "Submit" or "Send Reset Link."
  3. Check your inbox — A password reset email will arrive within a few minutes. Check your spam or junk folder if you don't see it in your primary inbox.
  4. Click the reset link — Open the email and click the secure link inside. This takes you to a page where you can create a new password.
  5. Create a strong new password — Use a mix of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Avoid using passwords you use on other sites — especially if those sites have ever had a data breach.
  6. Log in with your new password — Return to swapedly.com and sign in with your email and the new password.

If Your Reset Link Has Expired

Reset links are time-limited for security reasons — typically valid for one hour. If yours has expired, simply return to swapedly.com/#/forgot-password and request a fresh link.

Lost Access to Your Email Too?

If you no longer have access to the email address on your account, contact Swapedly support directly. Be prepared to verify your identity through alternative means — such as account details or prior transaction information.

Tip: Use a password manager (like 1Password, Bitwarden, or your browser's built-in manager) to generate and store a strong, unique password for Swapedly. You'll never need to reset it again.
Warning: If you receive a Swapedly password reset email that you didn't request, someone may have attempted to access your account. Change your password immediately and review your account for any unauthorized activity. Contact support if anything looks off.

faqs.q: How long is the reset link valid?
faqs.a: Reset links typically expire within one hour for security purposes. If yours has expired, request a new one at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password.

faqs.q: I didn't receive the reset email — what should I do?
faqs.a: Check your spam or junk folder first. If it's not there, verify you entered the correct email address and try again. Still nothing? Contact Swapedly support.

faqs.q: Can I reset my password if I no longer have access to my email?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support directly. They can help verify your identity through alternate means and assist with account recovery.

faqs.q: How often should I change my Swapedly password?
faqs.a: Change your password if you suspect unauthorized access, if you've reused the same password from another breached site, or as part of a regular personal security routine.

faqs.q: Can I reuse my old password when resetting?
faqs.a: Technically possible, but not recommended. Take the opportunity to create a fresh, strong, unique password for better security.

articles.title: Editing Your Profile
articles.summary: Update your Swapedly profile — photo, name, bio, and location — at any time from your account settings.
articles.content:
Keeping Your Profile Fresh

Your Swapedly profile is visible to every user who views your listings or considers transacting with you. Keeping it current and complete is one of the easiest ways to maintain buyer and seller confidence on the platform. Fortunately, editing your profile is quick and can be done at any time.

How to Edit Your Profile

  1. Log in to your Swapedly account.
  2. Navigate to your profile — Tap your name or avatar in the top navigation bar to open your profile page.
  3. Tap "Edit Profile" — Look for this button near your profile photo, name, or in the three-dot menu on your profile page.
  4. Update your information:
    • Profile photo — Tap your current photo to upload a new one. Use a clear, well-lit image of your face.
    • Display name — The name shown publicly to other Swapedly users.
    • Bio — Update your short description. Mention what you sell, your typical response time, and anything that helps others know what to expect when transacting with you.
    • Location — Update your city or region if you've moved or want to change your local pickup area.
  5. Save your changes — Tap "Save" or "Done" to apply the updates. Changes appear on your profile immediately.

What You Can't Edit in the Profile Section

Email address and password changes are handled separately for security reasons:

  • To change your email: see Changing Your Email Address
  • To change your password: see Resetting Your Password

Profile Visibility Reminder

Everything you enter in your profile — name, photo, bio, location — is visible to all Swapedly users. Never include personal contact information, phone numbers, or financial details in your bio. Your email address and wallet information are always kept private by the platform.

Tip: Revisit your bio every few months and update it to reflect what you're currently selling or looking for. An up-to-date bio signals an active user and makes a better impression on potential buyers and sellers.

faqs.q: Can I change my username?
faqs.a: Username changes may be limited or restricted to certain conditions. Contact Swapedly support if you need to update your username specifically.

faqs.q: Will editing my profile affect my active listings?
faqs.a: No. Your profile information is separate from listing details. Editing your profile doesn't change any listings.

faqs.q: Is there a limit on how often I can edit my profile?
faqs.a: No — you can update your profile as frequently as you like. There's no cool-down period or edit limit.

faqs.q: Can other users see my location when I edit it?
faqs.a: Only your general location (city or region) is publicly visible. Your specific address is never shown on your profile.

faqs.q: Do I need to re-verify anything after making profile changes?
faqs.a: Standard profile edits (photo, bio, name, location) don't require re-verification. Email changes are the exception — those require email verification.

articles.title: Changing Your Email Address
articles.summary: Update the email address on your Swapedly account securely — requires password confirmation and email verification.
articles.content:
When You Need to Change Your Email

Whether you're switching to a new email provider, correcting a typo from sign-up, or simply want a different address associated with your account, changing your Swapedly email is a secure, verified process. Because your email is both your login credential and your primary communication channel, Swapedly treats this change with extra care.

How to Change Your Email Address

  1. Go to Account Settings — Tap your profile, then navigate to Settings from the menu.
  2. Find the Email section — Look for "Email Address" or "Account Email" under account or security settings.
  3. Enter your new email address — Type the new address carefully. A typo here means you won't receive the verification link.
  4. Confirm with your current password — For security, Swapedly requires you to enter your current password to authorize an email change. This prevents unauthorized changes if someone gains brief access to your device.
  5. Check the new inbox for a verification email — Swapedly sends a confirmation link to the new email address. Open that email and click the link to finalize the change.
  6. Log in with your new email — After verification, your new email is active and replaces the old one as your login credential.

Troubleshooting

  • Verification email not arriving? — Check the spam/junk folder for the new email address. Verify you typed the new address correctly and try the process again.
  • Forgot your current password? — Reset it first at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password, then return to change your email.
  • No longer have access to the old email? — Contact Swapedly support directly. They can help verify your identity and assist with the transition.
Tip: Before changing your email, make sure you have full access to the new inbox. Open it in a separate browser tab so you're ready to click the verification link the moment it arrives.
Warning: If you receive an email saying your Swapedly email address was changed but you didn't initiate this, treat it as a potential security breach. Change your password immediately at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password and contact Swapedly support.

faqs.q: Do I need to verify the new email address?
faqs.a: Yes. Swapedly sends a verification link to the new email address. The change isn't finalized until you click that link.

faqs.q: Can two accounts share the same email address?
faqs.a: No. Each email address can only be associated with one Swapedly account at a time.

faqs.q: What if I no longer have access to my old email?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support. They can verify your identity through alternate means and help you regain access.

faqs.q: Will my old email address still work after the change?
faqs.a: Once the new email is verified, your old email is disassociated from the account. Use only the new email to log in going forward.

faqs.q: Is there a limit on how often I can change my email address?
faqs.a: Frequent email changes may trigger additional security verification. Contact support if you're experiencing issues with repeated changes.

articles.title: Managing Notifications
articles.summary: Control which Swapedly notifications you receive — and how — to stay informed about offers and sales without the noise.
articles.content:
Staying in the Loop Without Being Overwhelmed

Swapedly sends notifications for a wide range of events — new offers on your listings, incoming messages, completed sales, delivery confirmations, and more. The good news: you're in full control of which notifications you receive and how they're delivered. Here's how to set them up so you stay informed about what matters without drowning in alerts.

How to Access Notification Settings

  1. Log in and navigate to Settings from your profile or main menu.
  2. Tap "Notifications" or "Notification Preferences."
  3. Toggle individual notification types on or off.
  4. For each type, set your preferred delivery method: in-app only, email, push notification, or a combination.
  5. Save your preferences.

Notification Types Explained

  • New offer received — When a buyer makes an offer on one of your listings. Time-sensitive — enable this one.
  • New message — When another user sends you a message. Important for coordinating shipping, answering buyer questions, and handling offer negotiations.
  • Item sold — When a buyer completes a purchase of one of your listings. Always worth knowing right away so you can prepare to ship or schedule a pickup.
  • Delivery confirmed — When a buyer confirms receipt of your item, triggering your SB release. Great to have on so you know when your earnings hit your wallet.
  • Offer accepted / declined — Updates on offers you've made as a buyer. Keep this on to know whether to move on or proceed with a purchase.
  • Dispute update — Any update on an open dispute. Always keep this enabled if you have active disputes.
  • Promotional and platform announcements — News about new features, bonus SB promotions, and platform updates. Optional, but useful for not missing earning opportunities.
Tip: At minimum, keep offer and message notifications active. Missing a time-limited offer window because notifications were off is a frustrating and avoidable situation.
Warning: Turning off all notifications means you may miss accepted offers, incoming messages, and dispute updates. We recommend keeping transactional notifications enabled and only disabling purely promotional ones.

faqs.q: Can I turn off all notifications completely?
faqs.a: You can turn off most notification types, but we strongly recommend keeping offer, message, and dispute notifications enabled to avoid missing critical transaction events.

faqs.q: How do I stop receiving promotional emails?
faqs.a: Toggle off promotional or marketing notifications in your notification settings. You can also use the unsubscribe link at the bottom of any Swapedly marketing email.

faqs.q: Can I receive notifications by email instead of push?
faqs.a: Yes. For most notification types, you can choose between in-app, email, push notification, or a combination of delivery methods.

faqs.q: Why am I not receiving offer notifications even though they're enabled?
faqs.a: Check that your device's system settings allow push notifications from the Swapedly app. Also verify your email notifications aren't being filtered to spam.

faqs.q: Can I set quiet hours so I'm not notified late at night?
faqs.a: This depends on your device settings and app version. Check your device's "Do Not Disturb" or focus mode settings, or look for quiet-hours options in the Swapedly app's notification preferences.

articles.title: Deleting Your Account
articles.summary: If you need to leave Swapedly permanently, here's how to delete your account and what you need to do first.
articles.content:
Before You Delete: Consider Your Options

Account deletion on Swapedly is permanent and irreversible. Before taking this step, consider whether you simply want a break from the platform — in which case pausing your listings or temporarily deactivating may be a better fit. Contact support if you'd like to explore those options. If you're certain you want to close your account permanently, here's everything you need to know.

Required Steps Before Deleting

You cannot delete your account while active obligations are outstanding. Take care of the following first:

  • Complete or cancel all active transactions — Any purchase or sale in progress must be fully resolved. You cannot delete while SB are sitting in escrow.
  • Resolve any open disputes — Active disputes must be closed before deletion is possible.
  • Spend or accept the loss of your Swap Bucks — All SB remaining in your wallet at the time of deletion are permanently forfeited. They cannot be transferred, refunded, or converted to cash. Use them to buy something before deleting if you have a meaningful balance.
  • Cancel Swapedly Plus (if active) — Cancel your subscription to avoid future charges after your account is gone. See Cancelling Swapedly Plus.
  • Remove all active listings — Take down or mark sold any listings currently live in the marketplace.

How to Delete Your Account

  1. Go to Settings from your profile or main navigation.
  2. Scroll to the bottom of settings and look for "Delete Account" or "Close Account."
  3. Read the confirmation prompt carefully — it will outline what will be permanently removed.
  4. Confirm your decision by following the on-screen steps (usually entering your password).
  5. Your account and all associated data — listings, transaction history, reviews, SB balance — are permanently deleted.
Tip: If you're deleting because of a frustrating experience, consider reaching out to Swapedly support first. Many common issues can be resolved quickly, and the team genuinely wants to help.
Warning: Deletion is irreversible. Once your account is deleted, there is no recovery option. All your data, Swap Bucks, transaction history, and reviews are gone permanently.

faqs.q: Can I recover my account after deleting it?
faqs.a: No. Account deletion is permanent. Once processed, your data cannot be recovered under any circumstances.

faqs.q: What happens to my Swap Bucks when I delete my account?
faqs.a: All remaining SB in your wallet are permanently forfeited at the time of deletion. There is no refund, transfer, or cash-out option.

faqs.q: Do I need to cancel Swapedly Plus before deleting my account?
faqs.a: Yes. Cancel your Plus subscription first to prevent being charged after your account is deleted.

faqs.q: Can I temporarily deactivate my account instead of fully deleting it?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support to ask about temporary deactivation options if you want a break without permanent deletion.

faqs.q: Will my reviews and transaction history disappear when I delete?
faqs.a: Yes. All account data — including your reviews, transaction history, and listings — is permanently removed upon deletion.

title: Swapedly Plus
description: Swapedly's premium subscription — 1.5x Swap Bucks on sales, 5 monthly listing highlights, and unlimited purchases for $9.99/month.

articles.title: What Is Swapedly Plus?
articles.summary: Swapedly Plus is a $9.99/month subscription that boosts your SB earnings, gives you monthly listing highlights, and removes purchase limits.
articles.content:
The Premium Swapedly Experience

Swapedly Plus is a monthly subscription designed for users who want to get the maximum value out of the platform — whether that means earning more Swap Bucks on every sale, boosting the visibility of their listings, or buying as often as they want without restrictions. At $9.99 per month, it's built to pay for itself quickly for active sellers.

What's Included in Swapedly Plus

  • 1.5x Swap Bucks on all sales — The most impactful benefit. Every sale you make earns 50% more SB than the standard rate. Sell an item listed at 20 SB and receive 30 SB instead. This compounds fast if you sell regularly. See How the 1.5x Swap Bucks Boost Works for detailed examples.
  • 5 free listing highlights per month — Boost the visibility of your top listings in the marketplace. Highlights increase placement in search results and add a visual badge that catches buyer attention. These refresh every billing cycle. See Highlighting Your Listings.
  • Unlimited purchases — Buy as many items as you want with no purchase restrictions while your Plus subscription is active.

Is Swapedly Plus Worth It?

For active sellers, the math works quickly. If you sell 70 SB worth of items in a month, the 1.5x boost generates an extra 35 SB — $35 in marketplace value — on top of your standard earnings. That alone exceeds the $9.99 subscription cost by a wide margin.

Even for moderate sellers, the combination of boosted SB earnings and 5 monthly highlights creates tangible, measurable value that compounds over time.

How to Get Swapedly Plus

Upgrading takes just a few minutes. See How to Upgrade to Swapedly Plus for a step-by-step walkthrough.

Tip: Upgrade to Plus right before you plan to list a batch of items. That way, the 1.5x SB boost applies from your very first sale after upgrading, and you can use all 5 highlights on your new listings immediately.

faqs.q: How much does Swapedly Plus cost?
faqs.a: $9.99 per month, billed on a recurring monthly basis.

faqs.q: Can I cancel Swapedly Plus anytime?
faqs.a: Yes, you can cancel at any time with no cancellation fee. Your Plus benefits remain active through the end of your current billing period.

faqs.q: Does Swapedly Plus have a free trial?
faqs.a: Check the Swapedly Plus upgrade page for any current trial offers — these may be available for new subscribers.

faqs.q: What happens to my earnings and listings if I cancel Plus?
faqs.a: Your SB balance, listings, and account data are completely unaffected. You simply lose the Plus-specific benefits (1.5x boost, highlights, unlimited purchases) once the billing period ends.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade to Plus from the mobile app?
faqs.a: Yes. Swapedly Plus can be upgraded from both the mobile app and the website through the Settings menu.

articles.title: Swapedly Plus Benefits Explained
articles.summary: A detailed breakdown of every benefit included in Swapedly Plus and how to get the most out of each one.
articles.content:
Getting the Most from Your Plus Subscription

Understanding each Plus benefit in detail helps you use the subscription strategically — not just passively. Here's a deep dive into every benefit and how to maximize it.

Benefit 1: 1.5x Swap Bucks on Sales

This is the core financial benefit of Swapedly Plus. Every sale you complete while subscribed earns 50% more Swap Bucks than it would at the standard rate.

  • 10 SB sale → 15 SB received (standard would be 10)
  • 40 SB sale → 60 SB received (standard would be 40)
  • 100 SB sale → 150 SB received (standard would be 100)

The boost applies automatically to every completed sale — no action required on your end. It applies to the SB you receive, not to the price the buyer pays. Buyers always pay the listed price regardless of whether you're a Plus member.

See How the 1.5x Swap Bucks Boost Works for the full calculation guide and more examples.

Benefit 2: 5 Monthly Listing Highlights

Each billing cycle, you receive 5 listing highlights to use however you like. Highlights boost listing placement in search results and the marketplace feed, and add a visual indicator that catches buyer attention at a glance.

Key points about highlights:

  • They refresh at the start of every billing cycle
  • Unused highlights do not roll over to the next month
  • Use them on your highest-value, highest-urgency, or freshest listings for maximum impact
  • Highlights alone can meaningfully accelerate how fast your items sell

See Highlighting Your Listings for the how-to guide.

Benefit 3: Unlimited Purchases

Swapedly Plus removes any standard purchase limits, giving you unrestricted access to buy as many items as you want while your subscription is active. This is particularly useful for high-volume buyers who shop the marketplace frequently.

Standard vs. Plus at a Glance

  • SB earned per sale: 1x (standard) vs. 1.5x (Plus)
  • Free highlights/month: 0 (standard) vs. 5 (Plus)
  • Purchase access: Standard limits vs. unlimited (Plus)
  • Cost: Free vs. $9.99/month
Tip: Use all 5 monthly highlights before your billing cycle resets — they don't carry over. Set a reminder near your monthly renewal date to use any remaining highlights.

faqs.q: Do my 5 monthly highlights reset every month?
faqs.a: Yes. Your highlight allocation refreshes at the start of each new billing cycle.

faqs.q: Can I roll over unused highlights from one month to the next?
faqs.a: No. Unused monthly highlights expire when your billing cycle resets. Use them before the cutoff.

faqs.q: Does the 1.5x SB boost apply to items I listed before subscribing?
faqs.a: Yes. The boost applies to any sale completed while your Plus subscription is active, regardless of when you originally listed the item.

faqs.q: What happens to my benefits if I downgrade from Plus?
faqs.a: Your Plus benefits (1.5x boost, highlights, unlimited purchases) remain active through the end of your current paid billing period, then revert to standard.

faqs.q: Are there any other Plus perks beyond the three listed here?
faqs.a: Swapedly may add new Plus benefits over time. Check the Plus page in the app or on the website for the most current full list of included features.

articles.title: How to Upgrade to Swapedly Plus
articles.summary: Upgrade to Swapedly Plus in a few taps to unlock the 1.5x SB boost, monthly highlights, and unlimited purchases.
articles.content:
Ready to Go Plus?

Upgrading to Swapedly Plus is fast — the whole process takes about two minutes and your benefits activate the moment your payment is confirmed. Here's the step-by-step.

How to Upgrade

  1. Log in to your Swapedly account from the app or website.
  2. Go to Settings from your profile or main navigation menu.
  3. Find the Swapedly Plus section — Look for a "Swapedly Plus," "Upgrade," or "Go Plus" option in settings. On some versions, this may also appear as a prominent banner or button on the homepage.
  4. Review the subscription details — The upgrade page summarizes exactly what you get: 1.5x SB on sales, 5 highlights/month, and unlimited purchases, all for $9.99/month.
  5. Enter your payment information — Add a valid debit or credit card. Your card will be charged $9.99 immediately upon subscribing and on the same date each month going forward.
  6. Tap "Subscribe" or "Start Plus" — Confirm the subscription. Your benefits activate immediately — no waiting period.

What Happens Right After Upgrading

  • Your 1.5x SB boost is active on all new and existing listings right away
  • Your 5 monthly highlights become available to use immediately
  • Your account is marked as a Plus member, which may display a badge on your profile

Billing Details

You're charged $9.99 on the day you subscribe. Subsequent charges happen on the same calendar date each month. If your payment fails, Swapedly will typically retry and may suspend Plus benefits until payment is successful. Cancel anytime — see Cancelling Swapedly Plus.

Tip: The ideal time to upgrade is right before you plan to list several items for sale. You'll capture the 1.5x SB boost from the very first sale and can apply all 5 highlights to your newest listings immediately.
Warning: Make sure your payment method is current and has sufficient funds on your billing date. A failed payment may cause an interruption in your Plus benefits until the charge goes through successfully.

faqs.q: When do Plus benefits activate after I subscribe?
faqs.a: Immediately — as soon as your payment is confirmed, all three Plus benefits are active on your account.

faqs.q: Can I cancel right after subscribing to avoid next month's charge?
faqs.a: Yes. Cancelling after subscribing is allowed. Your Plus benefits stay active until the end of the current billing period.

faqs.q: What payment methods are accepted for Swapedly Plus?
faqs.a: Major debit and credit cards are accepted. Check the upgrade page for the most current list of supported payment methods.

faqs.q: Will I be notified before my Plus subscription auto-renews?
faqs.a: You may receive a renewal reminder notification. Enable billing alerts in your notification settings to stay informed.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade multiple accounts to Plus?
faqs.a: Each account requires its own Plus subscription. One subscription covers one account.

articles.title: How the 1.5x Swap Bucks Boost Works
articles.summary: Swapedly Plus members earn 1.5x Swap Bucks on every sale — here's exactly how it's calculated and when it applies.
articles.content:
Earn 50% More on Every Sale

The 1.5x Swap Bucks boost is the centerpiece financial benefit of Swapedly Plus. As a Plus subscriber, every item you sell earns 50% more Swap Bucks than the standard rate — automatically, on every completed transaction, with no extra steps required.

The Calculation

The boost is simple to understand:

  • Standard rate: Item price in SB × 1.0 = SB earned
  • Plus rate: Item price in SB × 1.5 = SB earned

Real examples:

  • Item priced at 10 SB → Standard earns 10 SB, Plus earns 15 SB
  • Item priced at 30 SB → Standard earns 30 SB, Plus earns 45 SB
  • Item priced at 60 SB → Standard earns 60 SB, Plus earns 90 SB
  • Item priced at 100 SB → Standard earns 100 SB, Plus earns 150 SB

When Is the Boost Applied?

The boost is applied when SB are released from escrow into your wallet — which happens when the buyer confirms delivery. You'll see the boosted amount reflected in your wallet transaction history with the full SB credit showing your Plus earnings. Your wallet will display the actual SB credited, not the pre-boost amount.

Important Details

  • Buyers are unaffected — The boost is on what you receive, not what buyers pay. Buyers always pay the listed price in SB. The 1.5x happens on the seller side only.
  • The boost is automatic — No codes to enter, no settings to toggle. Being a Plus subscriber is all it takes.
  • It covers all item types — The boost applies to every sale across every category while your subscription is active.
  • Transaction fee is unaffected — The 10% USD transaction fee (capped at $20) is calculated on the listed price, not the boosted SB amount. The boost only affects your SB earnings.
  • Timing matters — The boost applies to sales completed while Plus is active. If your subscription lapses before a buyer confirms delivery, the boost may not apply to that transaction.
Tip: Use the boost strategically. Before a period of heavy selling, make sure your Plus subscription is active and current. The more you sell, the more the 1.5x multiplier compounds into meaningful extra SB.

faqs.q: Does the 1.5x boost change the price buyers pay?
faqs.a: No. Buyers always pay the listed SB price. The boost increases what you receive as the seller, not what the buyer is charged.

faqs.q: Is the boost automatic, or do I need to enable it?
faqs.a: It's entirely automatic. Every sale completed while your Plus subscription is active earns the 1.5x rate — no action required on your part.

faqs.q: Does the 1.5x boost apply to referral bonuses or gift card SB?
faqs.a: No. The boost applies specifically to SB earned from selling items. Referral bonuses and gift card credits are at the standard 1 SB per unit rate.

faqs.q: What if my Plus subscription lapses mid-transaction?
faqs.a: If your subscription is no longer active when the buyer confirms delivery and SB are released, the boost may not apply to that sale. Keep your subscription current through active transactions.

faqs.q: Is there a monthly cap on boosted SB earnings?
faqs.a: There's no stated monthly cap. The 1.5x boost applies to every eligible sale completed during your subscription period, regardless of total volume.

articles.title: Cancelling Swapedly Plus
articles.summary: Cancel Swapedly Plus anytime with no fee — your benefits remain active through the end of your current billing period.
articles.content:
Cancelling Is Easy and Penalty-Free

Swapedly Plus has no cancellation fees and no lock-in commitment. You can cancel at any time, and your Plus benefits — the 1.5x SB boost, 5 monthly highlights, and unlimited purchases — stay active through the end of your current paid billing period. You're never cut off mid-month.

How to Cancel Swapedly Plus

  1. Log in to your Swapedly account on the app or website.
  2. Go to Settings from your profile or main navigation.
  3. Find the Swapedly Plus section — Look for "Swapedly Plus," "Manage Subscription," or "Subscription Settings."
  4. Tap "Cancel Subscription" or "Cancel Plus."
  5. Follow the on-screen confirmation prompts — Swapedly may ask for a reason for cancelling (optional) before finalizing.
  6. Note your benefits end date — The confirmation screen will show you the date through which your Plus benefits remain active.

What Happens After You Cancel

  • Your Plus benefits (1.5x boost, highlights, unlimited purchases) remain fully active until the end of your current billing period
  • No further charges are made after the current period
  • Your account reverts to standard (non-Plus) status at the billing period's end
  • Unused highlights from the final month expire when the period ends
  • Your SB balance, listings, transaction history, and all other account data remain completely intact

Timing Your Cancellation

Cancel a few days before your renewal date if you want to ensure you're not charged for another month. Your current-period benefits will still run their full course regardless of when within the period you cancel.

Tip: If you're cancelling because of a temporary change in your selling activity, consider whether you might want to re-subscribe later. Re-subscribing is just as easy as the original upgrade — see How to Upgrade to Swapedly Plus.
Warning: If you believe you were charged after cancelling, contact Swapedly support immediately with your cancellation confirmation details. Keep the confirmation email or screenshot as proof of your cancellation date.

faqs.q: Is there a cancellation fee for ending Swapedly Plus?
faqs.a: No. Cancellation is completely free. You can cancel at any time with no penalty.

faqs.q: What happens to my Swap Bucks and listings after I cancel?
faqs.a: Your SB balance, active listings, transaction history, and all account data are completely unaffected by cancelling Plus.

faqs.q: Can I re-subscribe to Swapedly Plus after cancelling?
faqs.a: Yes, absolutely. You can re-subscribe at any time through Settings. Any trial offers may not be available to returning subscribers.

faqs.q: Will I get a prorated refund for cancelling mid-month?
faqs.a: No. Swapedly Plus subscriptions are billed monthly and are non-refundable for the current period. Your benefits run through the end of the period you already paid for.

faqs.q: What if I was charged after I cancelled?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support immediately with your cancellation confirmation. Provide the date you cancelled and the charge date as reference.

title: Safety & Trust
description: Stay safe on Swapedly with escrow protection, dispute resolution tools, reporting features, and clear privacy controls.

articles.title: How Escrow Protection Works
articles.summary: Swapedly holds Swap Bucks in escrow during every transaction so neither buyer nor seller risks losing out.
articles.content:
What Is Escrow and Why Does It Matter?

Escrow is a financial arrangement where a neutral third party — in this case, Swapedly — holds funds securely while two parties complete an exchange. The funds are only released when both parties have fulfilled their obligations. On Swapedly, this means your Swap Bucks are held safely from the moment of purchase until you confirm you received what you paid for. Neither party can access or move the SB while a transaction is in progress.

The Escrow Flow, Step by Step

  1. Purchase initiated — The buyer taps "Buy Now." Their Swap Bucks are immediately transferred from their available wallet balance into a secure escrow hold. The buyer can no longer spend those SB; the seller cannot access them yet.
  2. Seller fulfills the order — The seller ships the item or arranges a local pickup meetup. The escrowed SB remain frozen throughout this phase, providing assurance to the seller that payment is secured and assurance to the buyer that payment hasn't been lost.
  3. Buyer receives the item — The item arrives by shipping or is handed over at a local meetup. The buyer inspects it carefully.
  4. Buyer confirms delivery — Satisfied with the item, the buyer confirms delivery in the Swapedly app. This is the trigger that releases the escrow.
  5. SB released to the seller — Immediately upon buyer confirmation, Swapedly releases the Swap Bucks to the seller's wallet. Both parties receive a confirmation notification. The transaction is complete.

How Escrow Protects the Buyer

As a buyer, escrow ensures that the seller cannot receive your Swap Bucks until you confirm you received the item as described. If a seller takes your payment and never ships, or ships something completely different, your SB remain frozen and protected while a dispute is investigated.

How Escrow Protects the Seller

As a seller, escrow assures you that the buyer's SB are committed from the moment of purchase. The buyer can't back out or claim they never paid. Before you ship or hand over the item, you know with certainty that the Swap Bucks are already secured.

Tip: Never confirm delivery before you've physically inspected the item. Once you confirm, the SB are released and your protection window closes. If anything is wrong, open a dispute first. See What to Do If Something Goes Wrong.
Warning: Escrow protection only applies to transactions completed entirely within Swapedly. Any deal made outside the platform — paying in cash, Venmo, or any other external method — receives no escrow protection whatsoever.

faqs.q: When are my Swap Bucks released to the seller?
faqs.a: Only after you confirm delivery in the Swapedly app. Until then, the SB remain in escrow and neither party can access them.

faqs.q: What if the seller never ships the item and I never confirm?
faqs.a: Open a dispute through the platform. Swapedly will investigate and the escrowed SB are not released to the seller until the case is resolved.

faqs.q: Is there a fee for escrow protection?
faqs.a: No. Escrow protection is built into every Swapedly transaction at no additional charge.

faqs.q: What happens to escrowed SB during an open dispute?
faqs.a: The SB remain completely frozen in escrow while Swapedly reviews the dispute. Neither party can access them until a resolution is reached.

faqs.q: Does escrow apply to local pickup transactions too?
faqs.a: Yes. As long as the transaction was initiated through Swapedly, escrow applies to both shipped and local pickup transactions.

articles.title: Reporting a User or Listing
articles.summary: If you encounter suspicious behavior or a policy-violating listing on Swapedly, here's how to report it and help keep the community safe.
articles.content:
Your Role in Keeping Swapedly Safe

Swapedly's moderation team works hard to maintain a trustworthy marketplace, but the community plays an equally important role. If you encounter behavior, a listing, or a user that seems suspicious, fraudulent, or in violation of platform rules, reporting it takes only a moment and genuinely helps protect other users from having a bad experience.

What You Can and Should Report

  • Listings for counterfeit, stolen, prohibited, or illegal items
  • Misleading or fraudulent listing descriptions or photos (stock images passed off as real item photos)
  • Sellers or buyers requesting payment or communication outside of Swapedly
  • Harassment, threats, or abusive behavior in messages
  • Suspected scam attempts or phishing messages
  • Duplicate or fake accounts
  • Any other behavior that violates Swapedly's community guidelines

How to Report a Listing

  1. Open the listing you want to report.
  2. Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) or look for a "Report" button on the listing detail page.
  3. Select the most accurate reason from the options provided (e.g., "Prohibited item," "Misleading description," "Suspected scam").
  4. Add any additional context in the description field — specific details help the moderation team act faster.
  5. Submit the report. You'll receive a confirmation that it was received.

How to Report a User

  1. Navigate to the user's profile page by tapping their name or photo.
  2. Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) or "Report" button on their profile.
  3. Select the reason and provide context.
  4. Submit.

After You Report

Reports are reviewed by Swapedly's moderation team, typically within 24–48 hours for serious cases. You won't be notified of the specific action taken (for privacy), but all reports are reviewed and appropriate action is taken. Depending on severity, outcomes range from a warning to listing removal to account suspension.

Tip: The more specific your report, the faster and more effectively the moderation team can respond. Include screenshots or reference specific messages when possible.
Warning: If a user is harassing or threatening you, stop all communication, block them, and report immediately. Do not attempt to resolve threatening behavior through negotiation.

faqs.q: Will the reported user know I filed a report?
faqs.a: No. Reports are completely anonymous — the reported user will never be told who submitted the report.

faqs.q: How long does it take for a report to be reviewed?
faqs.a: Swapedly's moderation team reviews reports as quickly as possible. Serious safety issues are typically prioritized. Complex policy cases may take longer.

faqs.q: What action does Swapedly take after I report someone?
faqs.a: Actions range from warnings and listing removal to temporary suspension or permanent banning, depending on the severity and evidence of the violation.

faqs.q: Can I report a user I've never transacted with?
faqs.a: Yes. You can report any user or listing on the platform that appears to violate Swapedly's policies, regardless of whether you've had a transaction with them.

faqs.q: What if I receive threatening or abusive messages from another user?
faqs.a: Report the user immediately using the report feature on their profile, then block them to prevent further contact. If you feel physically threatened, contact local law enforcement.

articles.title: Tips for Safe Local Meetups
articles.summary: When completing a local pickup in person, follow these safety tips to protect yourself every time.
articles.content:
Local Meetups Are Safe — with the Right Precautions

Thousands of Swapedly local pickup transactions happen every day without incident. Meeting a stranger to exchange goods is a normal part of community marketplace culture, and with a few sensible precautions, it's a very safe activity. Here's how to protect yourself every time.

Choose the Right Meeting Location

Where you meet is the single most important safety decision in a local pickup.

  • Meet in public, busy spaces — Coffee shops, shopping centers, grocery store parking lots, or any populated public area. The more foot traffic, the better.
  • Police station parking lots — Many police stations explicitly welcome and encourage marketplace exchanges in their parking areas. This is widely considered the gold standard for safe in-person transactions, and most sellers have no objection to meeting there.
  • Avoid private locations — Never meet at someone's home, a secluded area, or anywhere that isn't publicly visible. If a seller or buyer insists on a private location, that's a significant red flag.
  • Daytime is better — Schedule meetups during daylight hours when possible, especially for your first few exchanges with new contacts.

During the Meetup

  • Bring a friend or family member — Having company makes you safer and more confident. For high-value items especially, this is strongly recommended.
  • Let someone know where you're going — Tell a trusted person the location, the time, and the name of who you're meeting before you leave.
  • Keep your phone charged and accessible — You need it to confirm the transaction in the app, and you may need it in an emergency.
  • Inspect the item thoroughly — Take your time. Check every detail against the listing description and photos before confirming in the app. You're under no obligation to rush.
  • Confirm in the app before leaving — Complete the delivery confirmation while you're still at the meetup location. Don't leave and confirm later from home.
  • Trust your instincts — If something feels wrong — the location, the person's behavior, anything — you are always allowed to leave. Your safety is more important than any transaction.

Red Flags to Walk Away From

  • Pressure to meet immediately or at an unusual hour
  • Insistence on meeting at a private address
  • Request to pay in cash instead of through the Swapedly app
  • Vague or evasive answers to reasonable questions about the item
Warning: All payments must be processed through Swapedly, not in cash or via any outside method. Off-platform payment voids your escrow protection and is a common element of scams.

faqs.q: Can I insist on a specific meeting location?
faqs.a: Absolutely. You have every right to suggest or require a public meeting place. A trustworthy seller will have no objection to meeting at a coffee shop or police station lot.

faqs.q: What if the seller asks me to pay in cash at the meetup?
faqs.a: Decline firmly. All payments must go through Swapedly to maintain transaction protection. A seller requesting cash at a meetup should be reported.

faqs.q: What if the item looks different from the photos when I see it in person?
faqs.a: Don't confirm delivery. You have the right to walk away from the transaction. Open a dispute through the app — your escrowed SB are protected until it's resolved.

faqs.q: Should I bring ID to a local pickup?
faqs.a: It's not required, but in some situations it can help establish legitimacy and build mutual trust, especially for higher-value exchanges.

faqs.q: What are the safest types of places to meet?
faqs.a: Police station parking lots are considered the safest option. Busy coffee shops, bank lobbies, and shopping center common areas are also excellent choices.

articles.title: What to Do If Something Goes Wrong
articles.summary: If a transaction on Swapedly doesn't go as expected, here's exactly how to open and resolve a dispute to protect your Swap Bucks.
articles.content:
When Things Don't Go as Planned

The vast majority of Swapedly transactions complete smoothly, but occasionally something goes wrong — an item doesn't arrive, it's significantly different from the listing description, or a party fails to follow through on their commitment. When that happens, Swapedly's dispute process is how you get it resolved fairly, with your escrowed Swap Bucks protected throughout.

Step 1: Try to Resolve Directly First

Before opening a formal dispute, reach out to the other party via Swapedly messages. Explain the issue calmly and specifically. Many problems — a delayed shipment, a minor misunderstanding about condition, a missed meetup — can be resolved with a quick conversation. Give the other party a reasonable window (24–48 hours) to respond and address the issue.

Step 2: Open a Dispute if Direct Resolution Fails

If communication doesn't resolve the issue within a reasonable timeframe:

  1. Navigate to the transaction — Find it in your purchases or sales history.
  2. Tap "Open Dispute" or "Report an Issue" — This option appears on the transaction detail page, usually within the action menu.
  3. Select the reason for your dispute — Choose from options like "Item not received," "Item not as described," "Item arrived damaged," or other relevant categories.
  4. Provide supporting evidence — Include photos of what you received (vs. what was listed), screenshots of relevant messages, tracking information, and any other documentation that supports your case.
  5. Submit the dispute — Swapedly's moderation team is notified and begins reviewing.

What Happens During a Dispute

  • The escrowed Swap Bucks are frozen — neither party can access them while the dispute is under review.
  • Swapedly reviews evidence from both sides.
  • A resolution is reached: SB returned to the buyer, released to the seller, or a partial arrangement depending on the specifics of the case.
  • Both parties are notified of the outcome.
Tip: Keep all transaction communication within Swapedly messages. Off-platform communication (text, WhatsApp, etc.) cannot be reviewed by the moderation team and weakens your evidence in a dispute.
Warning: Do not confirm delivery before opening a dispute. Confirming delivery releases the seller's SB and signals that the transaction is complete. Disputes opened after confirmation are significantly harder to resolve in the buyer's favor.

faqs.q: How long do I have to open a dispute after a problem occurs?
faqs.a: There's a window after the expected delivery date during which disputes can be opened. Check your specific transaction page for the exact deadline — act before it expires.

faqs.q: What evidence should I submit in a dispute?
faqs.a: Photos of the item received, screenshots of the listing description and photos, your in-app messages with the other party, and any tracking or delivery documentation.

faqs.q: Can I open a dispute for a local pickup transaction?
faqs.a: Yes. The dispute process applies equally to shipped and local pickup transactions, as long as both were processed through Swapedly.

faqs.q: How long does Swapedly take to resolve a dispute?
faqs.a: Resolution time varies by case complexity. Clear-cut cases may be resolved in a few days; more complex situations may take longer as both sides are given time to respond.

faqs.q: What if I'm not satisfied with Swapedly's dispute resolution?
faqs.a: Contact Swapedly support to request a re-review and provide any additional evidence you didn't include in the original dispute. The team will take a second look.

articles.title: Your Privacy on Swapedly
articles.summary: Understand what Swapedly collects, what's visible to other users, and how your personal information is protected.
articles.content:
Privacy Built into the Platform

Swapedly is designed so that you can participate fully in the marketplace without exposing personal financial or contact information to other users. Here's a clear breakdown of what's public, what's private, and how your data is handled.

What Other Swapedly Users Can See

  • Your display name and profile photo
  • Your public bio
  • Your general location (city or region — never your exact address)
  • Your active listings and their details
  • Your completed transaction count and star rating
  • Written reviews left by buyers or sellers you've transacted with
  • Your member-since date

What Is Always Private

  • Your email address — never shown to other users
  • Your payment information and credit card details
  • Your Swap Bucks balance and transaction history
  • Your Purchase Credits balance
  • Your private message conversations
  • Your exact home or pickup address

What Swapedly Collects

Swapedly collects information necessary to operate the platform safely and effectively, including: account registration details, transaction history, device and browser information, usage data and activity on the platform, and payment processing data (handled by secure third-party payment processors — Swapedly does not store full card numbers). This data is used to provide the service, prevent fraud, resolve disputes, and improve the platform experience.

Controlling Your Data

  • Profile settings: Edit what appears publicly on your profile at any time via Edit Profile.
  • Notification preferences: Control how Swapedly contacts you via Notification Settings.
  • Account deletion: Request deletion of your account and associated data via Delete Account.
  • Full privacy policy: Read Swapedly's complete privacy policy at swapedly.com for detailed information on data handling, third-party sharing, and your legal rights.
Tip: Never share personal financial information — bank account details, credit card numbers, or Social Security numbers — with other Swapedly users via messages. Swapedly will never ask for this information through chat.
Warning: If you suspect your Swapedly account has been compromised or accessed without your permission, change your password immediately at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password and contact Swapedly support to report the incident.

faqs.q: Can other users see my purchase history?
faqs.a: No. Your individual purchase history is private. Other users can only see your total completed transaction count and any reviews left by your transaction partners.

faqs.q: Does Swapedly share my data with third parties?
faqs.a: Review Swapedly's full Privacy Policy at swapedly.com for complete details on data sharing practices with third parties.

faqs.q: Can I request deletion of all my personal data?
faqs.a: Deleting your account removes your data from the platform. For specific data requests, contact Swapedly support.

faqs.q: Are my messages on Swapedly private between me and the other user?
faqs.a: Messages are private between the two parties. Swapedly may access messages if a dispute or safety concern is reported and investigation is required.

faqs.q: What should I do if I think my account has been hacked?
faqs.a: Change your password immediately at swapedly.com/#/forgot-password, review your transaction history for unauthorized activity, and contact Swapedly support to report the incident.
